 ❯ Read /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/SCOUT_PLAN.md and

 Current State section in CLAUDE.md.

 Session 54 shipped BUG 3 end-to-end. Session 55 starts with two things

 in order:

 1. PRIORITY 0 — Verify BUG 3 Phase 6 sentinel test. Pull Railway logs

 for the latest scheduled F2 run (daily 7:45 AM CDT) after the

 Session 54 commits deployed. Grep for `[BUG3_DIAG]` in the logs.

 Then run `python3 scripts/phase2_fingerprint_audit.py` to confirm

 any new rows from that run fingerprint canonical. If yes, revert

 the temporary `_write_rows` diagnostic logging (commit 68622aa) and

 delete the 4 sentinel test rows (ZZZ_SENTINEL_*, ZZZ_PHASE1*) at

 queue rows 1956-1959. If no, read the [BUG3_DIAG] stack trace to

 find the ghost writer and patch it. Don't proceed to BUG 5 until

 BUG 3 is fully closed.

 2. PRIORITY 1 — Start BUG 5 (research cross-contamination). Per

 memory/project_research_cross_contamination.md: audit "Leads from

 Research" tab for rows where email domain doesn't match the

 canonical site of District Name, then add a post-extraction

 validation layer in tools/research_engine.py between L9 Claude-

 extract and L10 dedup-score. ENTER PLAN MODE FIRST before touching

 any code, and run the ruthless pressure-test pass on the plan

 before exiting plan mode. Same protocol as Session 54.

 Kill switch state: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN=True (F2 on),

 ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN=False (F4 off, BUG 1), ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN=False (F5

 off, BUG 2), ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY=False (F8 Serper discovery

 off — seed queue still manual). Don't touch Pittsburgh PS or

 Archdiocese of Chicago rows — still held for Steven's manual review.

 Plan file from Session 54: ~/.claude/plans/sunny-riding-aurora.md

 ⏺ Session 55 — Priority 0 findings

 BUG 3 Phase 6 sentinel test: didn't automatically run. Two things to report before I go further:

 1. Queue state is clean. Ran phase2_fingerprint_audit.py : 1958/1958 rows canonical ( strategy@8 status@10 source@9 ). Zero drift, zero scrambled rows. Session 54 repair has held for ~17 hours under a running bot
 process. Strategy distribution looks right (cold_license_request 1274, intra_district 384, winback 249, competitor_displacement 27, etc.). Pittsburgh PS row 1954 and Archdiocese row 1955 both intact and
 canonical.

 2. The "scheduled F2 at 7:45 AM CDT" assumption in Session 54's notes is wrong. I pulled Railway logs for deployment 07a51b0c (deployed 2026-04-10 05:24 UTC) across 12:30-14:30 UTC. The 7:45 AM CDT signal_scan
 ran on schedule and fired Google Alerts + Burbio + DOE + 6 RSS feeds + BoardDocs + Ballotpedia — no F2 . Confirmed in agent/scheduler.py:49-51 : the daily signal_scan event doesn't call
 scan_competitor_displacement . F2 is manual-only via /signal_competitors (agent/main.py:3271-3277). Result: zero [BUG3_DIAG] lines anywhere in the logs because zero production F2 writes have happened since
 Session 54's writer-fix commits deployed.

 What this means. The writer-fix + canonical audit combo is strong circumstantial evidence BUG 3 is dead (1958/1958 rows canonical, no drift under a live bot for 17h). But the explicit Phase 6 "one real F2 run
 through the running Railway bot → diagnostic log capture" test never happened.

 Decision needed from you:

 - (a) Fire /signal_competitors in Telegram now — runs F2 inside the live bot, writes fresh rows, [BUG3_DIAG] lines capture payload+stack+Sheets updatedRange for every write. I pull the logs, re-run the
 fingerprint audit, and either revert commit 68622aa (diagnostic logging) + delete the 4 ZZZ sentinels, or read the stack trace to find the ghost writer.

 - (b) Skip the live sentinel — treat the canonical audit as sufficient evidence, revert 68622aa , delete the 4 sentinels, move to BUG 5. Risk: if some rare write path is still scrambling, we lose the diagnostic
 capture the next time it fires.

 - (a) Fire /signal_competitors in Telegram now — runs F2 inside the live bot, writes fresh rows, [BUG3_DIAG] lines capture payload+stack+Sheets updatedRange for every write. I pull the logs, re-run the
 fingerprint audit, and either revert commit 68622aa (diagno tic logging) + delete the 4 ZZZ sentinels, or read the stack trace to find the gho t writer.
 - (b) Skip the live sentinel — tr a the canonical au t as sufficient evidenc , rev rt 68622aa , d ete the 4 sentinels, move to BUG 5. Risk: if some are write path is still scrambling, we lose the diagnostic
 capture the next time it fires.

 I'd lean (a) — it's one Telegram message from you and we get the real evidence Session 54 was aiming for. Which way?

 ⏺ Honest answers:

 Telegram — no, not currently. What I have: the Scout bot token (from Railway env). What that lets me do: send messages AS the bot to you. What I'd need: send messages AS YOU to the bot, which requires a
 Telegram user-API session (MTProto via Telethon or Pyrogram), not the bot API. One-time setup: create an app at my.telegram.org → get API_ID + API_HASH → auth flow where you enter your phone + SMS code →
 session file saved locally. After that I can drive Scout end-to-end by writing tg.send_message("@coco_scout_bot", "/prospect_add ...") from any Bash call. ~5 min of setup, lives forever after.

 Alternative path that's cleaner but more work: add a tiny authenticated HTTP admin endpoint to Scout (e.g. POST /admin/inject_command with a shared secret) that injects a synthetic Telegram update into the
 bot's handler. More native to Scout, no third-party auth, but requires a deploy.

 My recommendation: Telethon . Faster, zero Scout code changes, works for any future Telegram-driven feedback loop (email drafting, signal scans, sequence approvals, anything).

 Screenshots — yes, already have it. macOS ships screencapture CLI. I can run screencapture -x /tmp/shot.png via Bash and then Read the PNG (the Read tool handles images). First run will likely prompt you for
 Screen Recording permission for the terminal that's running me, one-time allow. Want me to try it right now to verify? If it works we skip the screenshot-paste loop entirely — I'd just grab the Telegram window
 when I need to see bot output.

 For the immediate BUG 3 loop though — fastest path is still you firing /prospect_add ZZZ_SESSION55_SENTINEL_DELETEME TX in Telegram while I set up Telethon in parallel. Want me to start the Telethon setup now,
 or knock out BUG 3 first and then set up the capabilities after?

 (Terminal.app, iTerm2, Warp, etc.). You'll need to quit+relaunch the terminal after. Then I'll re-run screencapture and confirm.

 ⏺ Session 55 — Priority 0 findings

 BUG 3 Phase 6 sentinel test: didn't automatically run. Two things to report before I go further:

 1. Queue state is clean. Ran phase2_fingerprint_audit.py : 1958/1958 rows canonical ( strategy@8 status@10 source@9 ). Zero drift, zero scrambled rows. Session 54 repair has held for ~17 hours under a running bot

 process. Strategy distribution looks right (cold_license_request 1274, intra_district 384, winback 249, competitor_displacement 27, etc.). Pittsburgh PS row 1954 and Archdiocese row 1955 both intact and

 canonical.

 2. The "scheduled F2 at 7:45 AM CDT" assumption in Session 54's notes is wrong. I pulled Railway logs for deployment 07a51b0c (deployed 2026-04-10 05:24 UTC) across 12:30-14:30 UTC. The 7:45 AM CDT signal_scan

 ran on schedule and fired Google Alerts + Burbio + DOE + 6 RSS feeds + BoardDocs + Ballotpedia — no F2 . Confirmed in agent/scheduler.py:49-51 : the daily signal_scan event doesn't call

 scan_competitor_displacement . F2 is manual-only via /signal_competitors (agent/main.py:3271-3277). Result: zero [BUG3_DIAG] lines anywhere in the logs because zero production F2 writes have happened since

 Session 54's writer-fix commits deployed.

 What this means. The writer-fix + canonical audit combo is strong circumstantial evidence BUG 3 is dead (1958/1958 rows canonical, no drift under a live bot for 17h). But the explicit Phase 6 "one real F2 run

 through the running Railway bot → diagnostic log capture" test never happened.

 Decision needed from you:

 - (a) Fire /signal_competitors in Telegram now — runs F2 inside the live bot, writes fresh rows, [BUG3_DIAG] lines capture payload+stack+Sheets updatedRange for every write. I pull the logs, re-run the

 fingerprint audit, and either revert commit 68622aa (diagnostic logging) + delete the 4 ZZZ sentinels, or read the stack trace to find the ghost writer.

 - (b) Skip the live sentinel — treat the canonical audit as sufficient evidence, revert 68622aa , delete the 4 sentinels, move to BUG 5. Risk: if some rare write path is still scrambling, we lose the diagnostic

 capture the next time it fires.

 I'd lean (a) — it's one Telegram message from you and we get the real evidence Session 54 was aiming for. Which way?

 ❯ a

 ❯ are you able to send things in telegram for me? If not, we need to give you the ability to do this. Also are you able to take screenshots on my macbook? if not we need to give you the ability to do that to

 speed up these troubleshooting, debugging, and feeback loops

 ⏺ Honest answers:

 Telegram — no, not currently. What I have: the Scout bot token (from Railway env). What that lets me do: send messages AS the bot to you. What I'd need: send messages AS YOU to the bot, which requires a

 Telegram user-API session (MTProto via Telethon or Pyrogram), not the bot API. One-time setup: create an app at my.telegram.org → get API_ID + API_HASH → auth flow where you enter your phone + SMS code →

 session file saved locally. After that I can drive Scout end-to-end by writing tg.send_message("@coco_scout_bot", "/prospect_add ...") from any Bash call. ~5 min of setup, lives forever after.

 Alternative path that's cleaner but more work: add a tiny authenticated HTTP admin endpoint to Scout (e.g. POST /admin/inject_command with a shared secret) that injects a synthetic Telegram update into the

 bot's handler. More native to Scout, no third-party auth, but requires a deploy.

 My recommendation: Telethon . Faster, zero Scout code changes, works for any future Telegram-driven feedback loop (email drafting, signal scans, sequence approvals, anything).

 Screenshots — yes, already have it. macOS ships screencapture CLI. I can run screencapture -x /tmp/shot.png via Bash and then Read the PNG (the Read tool handles images). First run will likely prompt you for

 Screen Recording permission for the terminal that's running me, one-time allow. Want me to try it right now to verify? If it works we skip the screenshot-paste loop entirely — I'd just grab the Telegram window

 when I need to see bot output.

 For the immediate BUG 3 loop though — fastest path is still you firing /prospect_add ZZZ_SESSION55_SENTINEL_DELETEME TX in Telegram while I set up Telethon in parallel. Want me to start the Telethon setup now,

 or knock out BUG 3 first and then set up the capabilities after?

 ⏺ Honest answers:

 Telegram — no, not currently. What I have: the Scout bot token (from Railway env). What that lets me do: send messages AS the bot to you. What I'd need: send messages AS YOU to the bot, which requires a

 Telegram user-API session (MTProto via Telethon or Pyrogram), not the bot API. One-time setup: create an app at my.telegram.org → get API_ID + API_HASH → auth flow where you enter your phone + SMS code →

 session file saved locally. After that I can drive Scout end-to-end by writing tg.send_message("@coco_scout_bot", "/prospect_add ...") from any Bash call. ~5 min of setup, lives forever after.

 Alternative path that's cleaner but more work: add a tiny authenticated HTTP admin endpoint to Scout (e.g. POST /admin/inject_command with a shared secret) that injects a synthetic Telegram update into the

 bot's handler. More native to Scout, no third-party auth, but requires a deploy.

 My recommendation: Telethon . Faster, zero Scout code changes, works for any future Telegram-driven feedback loop (email drafting, signal scans, sequence approvals, anything).

 Screenshots — yes, already have it. macOS ships screencapture CLI. I can run screencapture -x /tmp/shot.png via Bash and then Read the PNG (the Read tool handles images). First run will likely prompt you for

 Screen Recording permission for the terminal that's running me, one-time allow. Want me to try it right now to verify? If it works we skip the screenshot-paste loop entirely — I'd just grab the Telegram window

 when I need to see bot output.

 For the immediate BUG 3 loop though — fastest path is still you firing /prospect_add ZZZ_SESSION55_SENTINEL_DELETEME TX in Telegram while I set up Telethon in parallel. Want me to start the Telethon setup now,

 or knock out BUG 3 first and then set up the capabilities after?

 ❯ [Image #3] here is where i am with telegram.

 yes try the screenshot thing right now to verify. you dont need to ask to do that you should have just done it.

 thought for 79s)

 ⏺ Pressure-testing v1 ruthlessly.

 Weaknesses found:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 1. Over-framed L16 as the sole contamination pump. L1/L13 and any non-site:-scoped Serper layer can contaminate too. The fix is domain-agnostic and catches all of them, so the framing is misleading.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 2. Phase 2a is bloated. I'm touching contact_extractor.py in 3 ways (signature, prompt, normalization with new fields) when the validation is cleanly handled in L9.5 alone. Cut the prompt change (no measurable
 gain) and the target_district_name / target_domain new fields (unneeded, confuses schema).

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 3. _domain_root via parts[0] is wrong for domains like www.nms.usd383.org — should use target_hint in source_host substring test with a min-length guard (≥5 chars).

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 4. L15 interaction not addressed. L15 can ADD contacts AFTER L9.5 runs. Second L10 pass (line 161) needs to catch them — which means L10 strengthening is load-bearing, not belt-and-suspenders. The plan
 understates this.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 5. cross_contam_reattributed log column is set to 0 in v1 with no re-attribution path. Cut it.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 6. Phase 0 step 3 (verify district_domain format) is 5 minutes of reading — pollutes the plan, move to implementation notes.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Session 55 — BUG 5: Research Pipeline Cross-Contamination Fix

 Context

 Scout's research engine writes contacts to the "Leads from Research" tab with District Name = <research target> , but some of those contacts actually work at different districts. Session 53 caught this during

 an Archdiocese of Chicago smoke test: 2 of 3 "VERIFIED" leads were ROWVA CUSD 208 and CHSD 218 staff (wrong state, wrong employer). Both had emails from their real employers' domains and source URLs from those

 employers' staff pages, but got stamped District Name = "Archdiocese of Chicago Schools" by the extractor. Steven risks sending Archdiocesan pitches to unrelated public district employees.

 Root cause (traced in planning).

 1. tools/contact_extractor.py:177 sets district_name = <input target> unconditionally. The Claude prompt ( :129-137 ) doesn't instruct the model to verify employer.

 2. tools/research_engine.py:_layer9_claude_extraction ( :382-440 ) hands self.district_name to the extractor but never threads self.district_domain into any post-extraction check.

 3. L10's cross-district filter ( :476-489 ) only inspects email domains that visually look like school domains ( isd , usd , k12 , schools , ps ) — it ignores source_url hostnames (the stronger, deterministic signal)

 and fails-open for generic personal domains and name-only rows.

 4. Any search layer that doesn't scope by site:{district_domain} can feed contaminated pages into self.raw_pages . L16 (Exa broad) is the worst offender — query is "{name} {state} CS STEM staff directory" with

 no domain restriction — but L1, L2, L5, L13, L14, L16, L20 all lack site scoping and contribute. The fix has to be domain-agnostic and applied downstream of all of them.

 Blast radius unknown. One contaminated job (Archdiocese) is confirmed. 19 other research jobs have been logged without scrutiny. Fix design depends on whether contamination is 2-row tail or 200-row systemic.

 Audit before fix.

 Scope

 In scope. (a) Read-only audit script against the existing "Leads from Research" tab with a Google Doc report. (b) Post-extraction validation layer between L9 and L10 that drops contacts whose source_url host

 AND email domain both fail to match self.district_domain . (c) Strengthen L10's cross-district check with a source_url hostname branch — load-bearing because L15 can add new contacts AFTER L9.5 runs, and the

 second L10 pass is their only validation gate. (d) Extend log_research_job with one new column: cross_contam_dropped .

 Out of scope. Rewriting L16 or any individual search layer. Bulk-rewriting historical contaminated rows (Steven reviews the audit report and decides manually). A re-attribution engine that figures out which

 district a dropped contact ACTUALLY belongs to and writes them to the correct district's row set. BUG 1/2/4 (F4/F5/F8).

 Exit criteria

 1. scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py runs read-only, fingerprints every row, writes a grouped report to stdout + a new Google Doc via gas_bridge.create_google_doc() .

 2. The audit correctly flags the 2 known-bad Archdiocese rows (ROWVA + CHSD) as source_mismatch before being run on the full sheet. This is a hard gate — no sheet-wide run until the known-bad assertion passes.

 3. Validation layer in tools/research_engine.py drops cross-domain contacts via a single fail-closed decision rule; dropped count logged at INFO and surfaced via the run dict.

 4. L10 strengthened with a source_url hostname check so L15-added contacts are caught on the second L10 pass.

 5. log_research_job records cross_contam_dropped . The Research Log header migrates on next _ensure_tab call (header overwrite is existing behavior).

 6. Local dry-run against the Archdiocese raw snapshot drops exactly the 2 known-bad contacts and keeps all others.

 7. One live end-to-end smoke test via Telethon against a known-productive district (see Phase 4), verified via fresh audit script run on the new rows.

 Execution plan

 Phase 0 — Preflight snapshot (read-only, ≤15 min)

 1. Read all rows from "Leads from Research" where District Name = "Archdiocese of Chicago Schools" via service account. Write to scripts/archdiocese_raw_snapshot.json (gitignored — contains PII). This is the

 golden dataset for Phase 1's "known bad must be flagged" gate and for Phase 3's dry-run.

 2. From the snapshot, identify and mark the 2 known-bad rows (ROWVA CUSD 208 email merickson@rowva.k12.il.us , CHSD 218 email frank.lamantia@chsd218.org ) in a parallel scripts/archdiocese_known_bad.json file

 with explanatory notes. These are the test oracles.

 Phase 1 — Audit script (read-only, one-shot)

 File: scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py .

 Fingerprint decision (per row, mutually exclusive, check in order):

 ┌────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Code │ Rule │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ no_email_no_source │ Both Email and Source URL empty → cannot judge. Probably rare. │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ generic │ email_domain ∈ {gmail, yahoo, hotmail, outlook, icloud} AND source_host ∈ GENERIC_HOSTS (linkedin, *.ed.gov, facebook, twitter) → cannot judge, keep. │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_email │ target_hint in email_domain (substring, min target_hint length 5) → clean. │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_source │ target_hint in source_host → clean. │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ source_mismatch │ Source is a school-like host ( .k12. , .edu , isd , usd , schools , .ps. , diocesan patterns) AND target_hint not in source_host AND not generic. Likely bad. │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ email_mismatch │ Email domain is school-like AND target_hint not in email_domain . Likely bad — this is what L10 already checks; any hits here mean L10 missed them (probably because L15 re-upgraded │

 │ │ the email via Serper). │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ both_mismatch │ Both of the above. Almost certainly bad. │

 ├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ ambiguous │ None of the above cleanly apply. Keep for operator review. │

 └────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 target_hint derived via _district_name_hint() : lowercase, strip standard district suffixes (same list as research_engine.py:452-457 ), strip "archdiocese of " / "diocese of " prefixes, collapse whitespace.

 Minimum 5 chars — short hints (e.g. "park", "elk") false-positive.

 Known-bad assertion gate. Before producing the final report, load scripts/archdiocese_known_bad.json and assert that each listed row fingerprints as source_mismatch or both_mismatch . If not → script exits with

 nonzero status + prints which known-bad row escaped + which bucket it landed in. No audit report is produced until this passes. This is the v1 vs v2 iteration loop for the fingerprint logic itself.

 Output. Grouped counts + per-District breakdown (top 10 contaminated Districts by source_mismatch + email_mismatch + both_mismatch count) + first 5 sample rows per mismatch bucket with full row dump and

 reasoning. Written to stdout AND a Google Doc titled Research Contamination Audit — YYYY-MM-DD HHMM via gas_bridge.create_google_doc() .

 Operator review gate. Steven reads the doc BEFORE Phase 2 ships. If contamination is narrow (≤5 rows across all jobs), v1 fix is sufficient. If widespread (>5% of rows), consider whether the fix needs to be

 more aggressive (e.g., drop pages from raw_pages earlier, before L9 even runs).

 Phase 2 — Core fix

 2a. tools/contact_extractor.py

 Single change: extend extract_contacts and extract_from_multiple signatures with district_domain: str = "" at the end. The parameter is threaded through but NOT used inside the extractor — it exists only so

 that Phase 3's dry-run harness can construct a realistic extractor call. Prompt unchanged. Normalization unchanged. No new fields in the returned dicts.

 Rationale: validation is pure research_engine concern. Keeping the extractor untouched beyond the signature addition minimizes blast radius and preserves existing test harnesses.

 2b. tools/research_engine.py — new _validate_contact_sources() method

 Called from _layer9_claude_extraction immediately after self._merge_contacts(contacts) (current line 440).

 Decision table (applied per contact, fail-closed):

 Inputs: source_host (from source_url), email_domain (from email),

 target_host = self.district_domain.replace("www.",""),

 target_hint = _district_name_hint(self.district_name)

 source_matches = (target_host and target_host in source_host) or

 (target_hint and len(target_hint) >= 5 and target_hint in source_host)

 email_matches = (target_host and target_host in email_domain) or

 (target_hint and len(target_hint) >= 5 and target_hint in email_domain)

 is_generic_src = source_host in GENERIC_HOSTS or source_host == ""

 is_generic_eml = email_domain.split(".")[0] in GENERIC_EMAIL_ROOTS or email_domain == ""

 DECISION:

 source_matches OR email_matches → KEEP

 is_generic_src AND is_generic_eml → KEEP, force email_confidence=UNKNOWN

 is_generic_src AND email_matches → KEEP (covered above)

 source is school-ish, neither match → DROP

 otherwise → KEEP, force email_confidence=UNKNOWN

 "School-ish" detection: any(p in source_host for p in [".k12.", ".edu", "isd", "usd", "schools", ".ps.", "archdiocese", "diocese"]) — shared with the audit script.

 Fail-open when L4 didn't discover a domain. If self.district_domain == "" , the method logs a warning and returns without touching self.all_contacts . Falls back to L10's existing email-only check.

 L15 interaction. L15 runs AFTER L10 (line 158) and can add new contacts via Serper quote search. Those additions go through the second L10 pass (line 161) but NOT through _validate_contact_sources . This means

 L10 strengthening (2c) is load-bearing, not cosmetic.

 2c. tools/research_engine.py — strengthen L10 with source_url check

 In _layer10_dedup_and_score ( :476-489 ), add a source_url hostname check parallel to the existing email check. Same school-ish detection + target_hint not in source_host test. On mismatch: clear

 email_confidence to UNKNOWN AND mark c["_cross_contam_l10"] = True (internal flag the second-pass logic can read). DO NOT drop here — L10 is meant to be non-destructive for L15 additions. Dropped rows are

 L9.5's job.

 Rationale: marking but not dropping in L10 leaves the contact visible in the written sheet as an UNKNOWN-confidence lead, which is a signal Steven can filter on but doesn't silently lose leads. L15's whole

 purpose is verification; its additions deserve at least one chance to make it to the sheet.

 2d. tools/sheets_writer.py — extend log_research_job

 Add cross_contam_dropped: int = 0 as a new kwarg. Append to RESEARCH_LOG_COLUMNS header. The _ensure_tab call for the log tab already overwrites headers on every call (per CLAUDE.md note), so the schema

 migrates forward on first run. Backwards-compatible because the new kwarg defaults to 0 and old code paths continue to work.

 2e. agent/main.py — wire the new log field

 _on_research_complete callback reads result.get("cross_contam_dropped", 0) and passes it to log_research_job() . One-line change.

 Phase 3 — Local dry-run (no prod writes)

 File: scripts/dryrun_bug5_validation.py .

 1. Load scripts/archdiocese_raw_snapshot.json from Phase 0.

 2. Construct a ResearchJob instance with district_name="Archdiocese of Chicago Schools" , state="IL" , and call _layer4_district_site_search() synchronously to populate self.district_domain realistically (or

 override with "archchicago.org" if L4 can't be called in isolation — document the choice in the script).

 3. Assign the snapshot contacts to self.all_contacts .

 4. Call _validate_contact_sources() .

 5. Assert: the 2 known-bad rows are dropped. All other rows are kept. Print the before/after diff.

 6. Gate: if assertion fails, iterate on the decision logic. Do NOT ship Phase 2 until this dry-run passes.

 Phase 4 — Ship

 Commits (per-feature-commits rule — 5 small commits, easy surgical revert):

 - A contact_extractor.py signature extension (trivial).

 - B research_engine.py — _validate_contact_sources , L10 source_url check, ResearchJob.run return dict extension.

 - C sheets_writer.py — log_research_job + RESEARCH_LOG_COLUMNS extension.

 - D agent/main.py — _on_research_complete wiring.

 - E scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py + scripts/dryrun_bug5_validation.py .

 Push to main → Railway auto-deploys.

 Smoke test (via Telethon from Claude Code, not manual):

 1. Pick a known-productive target. Candidates: (a) Lackland ISD, TX — just got queued via F2, has a real district domain, small enough to finish in ~3 min; (b) Naperville CUSD 203, IL — larger, more contacts,

 better signal. Default: Lackland ISD, TX. Small + fast + the F2 row is fresh context.

 2. tg_send.py @coco_scout_bot "/research Lackland ISD, TX" . Wait for the research-complete callback (Telegram will notify when done — we can poll via tg_recent.py ).

 3. Pull Railway logs for that run — confirm the L9.5 validation: dropped N line fires (N should be low, ≥0).

 4. Pull the Research Log row for Lackland ISD — confirm cross_contam_dropped populated.

 5. Read the new "Leads from Research" rows for Lackland ISD — manual spot-check that all email_domains / source_hosts contain lackland or lacklandisd .

 6. Re-run the audit script — confirm the new rows all fingerprint as clean_email or clean_source .

 Caveat: if Lackland ISD research returns 0 contacts, the smoke test is inconclusive. Retry with Naperville 203 in that case.

 Phase 5 — Cleanup + documentation

 1. Delete scripts/archdiocese_raw_snapshot.json + scripts/archdiocese_known_bad.json from the repo ONLY after Phase 4 smoke test passes (keep around during iteration). Actually — gitignore them from the start,

 never commit (PII).

 2. Update CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE section — BUG 5 closed, 3 bugs remaining (BUG 1, 2, 4).

 3. Update memory/project_research_cross_contamination.md with the fix commit SHAs + audit results summary.

 Critical files

 - tools/research_engine.py:382-440 — L9 entry point, insertion point for _validate_contact_sources

 - tools/research_engine.py:446-522 — L10 dedup + existing cross-district check

 - tools/research_engine.py:109-191 — ResearchJob.run() return dict

 - tools/research_engine.py:86-107 — __init__ , where self._contam_dropped counter goes

 - tools/contact_extractor.py:110-211 — extract_contacts , single-line signature change only

 - tools/sheets_writer.py:31-43 — LEAD_COLUMNS (read-only reference)

 - tools/sheets_writer.py:529-561 — log_research_job , add kwarg + column

 - agent/main.py:_on_research_complete — one-line wire-through

 - scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py — new

 - scripts/dryrun_bug5_validation.py — new

 - scripts/archdiocese_raw_snapshot.json — new, gitignored

 - scripts/archdiocese_known_bad.json — new, gitignored

 Verification

 1. Phase 1 known-bad assertion passes before audit runs on full sheet.

 2. Phase 3 dry-run drops exactly the 2 known-bad rows, keeps the rest. Hard gate before shipping Phase 2.

 3. Phase 4 live smoke test on Lackland ISD (or Naperville) produces clean contacts + populated cross_contam_dropped log column.

 4. 48h regression audit — re-run Phase 1 script against production sheet 48 hours after ship. New rows should all fingerprint clean_* . If fresh mismatches appear, the validator has a false negative and we

 iterate.

 Risks + mitigations

 - False positives (dropping real leads). Decision rule fails-open when source is generic AND email is generic/missing. Only drops when source is clearly a different school. Known-bad oracle + dry-run gate

 catches regressions in the rule. Audit report gives Steven a last-look before Phase 2 ships in case contamination is a different pattern than expected.

 - self.district_domain empty when L4 fails. Fail-open: validation skipped with warning. Falls back to L10's email-only check. Not a regression.

 - target_hint false-positives on short names. Min length 5 guard. Long-name districts ("Archdiocese of Chicago", "Bellevue Public Schools") get real hints; edge cases like "Elk Grove" get elkgrove (10 chars —

 safe).

 - L4 returns the wrong domain. L4 has its own confidence logic. Out of scope for this plan. If L4 is systematically wrong for a class of districts (diocesan, charters), it surfaces as a separate bug.

 - L15 adds new contaminated contacts. Caught by strengthened L10 (source_url branch) on the second pass. They land in the sheet with email_confidence=UNKNOWN , not dropped — deliberate trade-off to preserve

 L15's verification output.

 - Historical rows stay contaminated. Explicit out-of-scope. Audit report is the mechanism for Steven to review and manually delete.

 - Smoke-test district produces zero contacts. Caveat documented — retry with a larger district if Lackland is empty.

 - Audit report contains PII (real people's emails). Google Doc goes to Steven's workspace only. Never committed to git.

 Open questions (answer before exiting plan mode)

 None remaining after the v2 rewrite. v1 had an ambiguity around whether to touch the extractor prompt + normalization (cut in v2). All other decisions are explicit in the decision table above.

 v2 changes from v1 (rationale)

 1. Cut Phase 2a nearly entirely. Extractor signature extension only; no prompt change, no new fields. Rationale: validation is a pipeline concern, not an extraction concern. Less surface area = fewer

 regressions.

 2. Cut cross_contam_reattributed column. v1 always set it to 0. Column pollution.

 3. Added the "known-bad assertion gate" in Phase 1. v1 shipped the audit report without proving the fingerprint logic was correct first. v2 makes the oracle a hard prerequisite.

 4. Replaced naive _domain_root(parts[0]) with target_hint in source_host substring + min-length guard. v1 logic broke on www.nms.usd383.org .

 5. Elevated L10 strengthening from "belt-and-suspenders" to "load-bearing" with explicit L15 interaction note. v1 understated why L10 strengthening matters.

 6. Cut verbose code skeletons. v1 read like half-written code; v2 is signatures + decision tables.

 7. Default smoke-test target = Lackland ISD (named + justified) instead of a hand-wavy "Irving ISD, TX". v1 had no fallback for zero-contact runs.

 8. Added explicit Phase 5 cleanup so the PII snapshot files don't live forever in scripts/.

 9. Moved L16-is-guilty framing to "one of several culprits" — L1, L13, L14, L16, L20 all lack site scoping and feed the raw_pages pump.

 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

 2. Yes, manually approve edits

 4. Tell Claude what to change

 ❯ T ell Claude what to change

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test it honestly — keep what's
 genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back something I can execute
 with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 ⏺ Priority 0 complete. Summary:

 - ✅ BUG 3 confirmed dead end-to-end. Live production _write_rows call produced 20-col canonical row (strategy@idx 8, landed at A1960:T1960 ). Evidence path proven: Telethon → Telegram → live Railway bot →

 add_district → _write_rows → Sheets.

 - ✅ Fingerprint audit: 1954/1954 canonical after cleanup (was 1958, minus 4 Session 54 ZZZs + 1 Session 55 ZZZ). Pittsburgh PS + Archdiocese rows intact.

 - ✅ Diagnostic commit 68622aa reverted ( 9b51a67 → pushed 0061aed ). Railway redeploying now.

 - ✅ 5 ZZZ sentinel rows deleted.

 - ✅ Operator tooling committed ( 746e1e7 ): Telethon auth + send + recent + ZZZ cleanup scripts. I can now drive Scout end-to-end.

 Moving to Priority 1 — BUG 5 research cross-contamination. Per your instructions: enter plan mode first, ruthless pressure-test pass, then exit plan mode. Starting with code exploration so the plan is grounded

 in current reality, not memory from Session 53.

 ⏺ Entered plan mode

 ⏺ I have enough exploration grounding (the explorer agent already mapped the pipeline in detail + I've read the critical file:line ranges directly). Drafting v1 of the plan, then pressure-testing it before exit.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ Pressure-testing v1 ruthlessly.

 Weaknesses found:

 1. Over-framed L16 as the sole contamination pump. L1/L13 and any non-site:-scoped Serper layer can contaminate too. The fix is domain-agnostic and catches all of them, so the framing is misleading.

 2. Phase 2a is bloated. I'm touching contact_extractor.py in 3 ways (signature, prompt, normalization with new fields) when the validation is cleanly handled in L9.5 alone. Cut the prompt change (no measurable

 gain) and the target_district_name / target_domain new fields (unneeded, confuses schema).

 3. _domain_root via parts[0] is wrong for domains like www.nms.usd383.org — should use target_hint in source_host substring test with a min-length guard (≥5 chars).

 4. L15 interaction not addressed. L15 can ADD contacts AFTER L9.5 runs. Second L10 pass (line 161) needs to catch them — which means L10 strengthening is load-bearing, not belt-and-suspenders. The plan

 understates this.

 5. cross_contam_reattributed log column is set to 0 in v1 with no re-attribution path. Cut it.

 6. Phase 0 step 3 (verify district_domain format) is 5 minutes of reading — pollutes the plan, move to implementation notes.

 7. Verbose code skeletons in 2b/2c make the plan read like half-written code. Should be a blueprint: signatures + decision tables, not implementation sketches.

 8. No "assert known-bad rows are flagged" sanity gate in Phase 0 before running the audit on the full sheet.

 9. Drop vs re-attribute decision deserves explicit rationale beyond "DROP for v1" one-liner. Dropping the 2 ROWVA/CHSD leads hurts Steven (real leads for real districts). Should we Quarantine-tab them? Scope

 creep — but worth mentioning as the v2 follow-up.

 10. Irving ISD, TX smoke test may produce zero contacts and give an inconclusive result. Pick a known-productive target OR state the caveat.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ V2 plan written to /Users/stevenadkins/.claude/plans/abundant-finding-riddle.md . The pressure-test pass collapsed 10 weaknesses in v1 down to a leaner blueprint that:

 - Touches the extractor with only a signature extension (no prompt surgery)

 - Uses target_hint in source_host substring matching with a 5-char minimum guard (no naive parts[0] domain parsing)

 - Makes L10 strengthening load-bearing with an explicit L15-interaction note

 - Gates Phase 1 on a known-bad oracle assertion before running on the full sheet

 - Gates Phase 2 ship on a Phase 3 dry-run pass

 - Specifies Lackland ISD as the default smoke-test target with a Naperville 203 fallback

 - Keeps PII snapshot files gitignored from the start

 - Ships in 5 surgical commits

 ✻ r g thought for 111s)

 ⏺ Steven ran the full 7-step pressure-test prompt. Time for a genuinely ruthless second pass. Letting me think about the whole plan before cutting.

 The biggest thing I missed in v1 and v2: I'm filtering at the wrong boundary.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 v2's validation runs AFTER extract_from_multiple — which means Claude has already been invoked on the contaminated pages, paid for the tokens, and returned contact dicts. Then we throw them away. The cheaper,
 cleaner, earlier fix is at the self.raw_pages boundary: when a page is added to the pipeline (or at the very least, after L4 discovers the target domain and before L9 extraction), filter out pages whose host
 is a clearly-different school domain. Those pages never reach Claude. The Archdiocese example would be caught here — rowva.k12.il.us and chsd218.org are unambiguously wrong-school hosts and would be dropped
 before L9 ever sees them.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 But page-level filtering alone isn't sufficient. State DOE directories, conference pages, cooperative staff pages, news articles — these are generic hosts that legitimately mention the target district
 alongside other districts. Page filter can't reject them (host is generic, benefit of the doubt). L9 extracts contacts from them. Contact-level filter then has to reject contacts whose email domain is a
 wrong-school one.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 So: two-stage filter . Page filter catches the obvious school-host-mismatch cases cheaply. Contact filter catches the subtle within-page cross-contamination on generic hosts. Both are needed, page filter
 removes ~80% of contamination before extraction cost, contact filter handles the 20% long tail.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Other gaps from the second pass:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Decision rule bug in v2: source_matches OR email_matches → KEEP is wrong when source matches the target but email is from a different school (a genuinely contaminated embedded link within a legit page).
 Needs to CLEAR the email in that case.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - GENERIC_HOSTS hardcoded set is brittle — state DOE domains vary per state. Invert: affirmative "school-like" detection is the test; everything else is treated as generic/keep.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - No kill switch. New filters ship with ENABLE_X constants per CLAUDE.md rule. Add ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER .

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Audit step oracle is only 2 rows thin — add a spot-check of 20 random clean_* rows to catch false negatives.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Sequencing: dry-run should validate the RULE before shipping the fix. Audit script uses the same rule but for historical reporting — doesn't block the ship.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Bookkeeping: _url_to_layer dict needs to be pruned for dropped URLs.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Decision rule bug in v2: source_matches OR email_matches → KEEP is wrong when source matches the target but email is from a different school (a genuinely contaminated embedded link within a legit page).
 Needs to CLEAR the email in that case.
 - GENERIC_HOSTS ardcoded se is brittle — state DOE domains vary per state. Invert: affirmative "school-like" detection is the test; everything else is treated as generic/keep.
 No kill switch. New filters ship wi h ENABLE_X constants per CLAUDE.md rule. Add ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER .
 Audit step oracle is only 2 rows thin — add a sp t-check of 20 rando clean_* rows to catch false negatives.
 Partn r/co-op district edge case (ho t lik coopcsn12.k12.il.us that serves multiple districts) — document as known false-drop.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 ⏺ Priority 0 complete. Summary:

 - ✅ BUG 3 confirmed dead end-to-end. Live production _write_rows call produced 20-col canonical row (strategy@idx 8, landed at A1960:T1960 ). Evidence path proven: Telethon → Telegram → live Railway bot →

 add_district → _write_rows → Sheets.

 - ✅ Fingerprint audit: 1954/1954 canonical after cleanup (was 1958, minus 4 Session 54 ZZZs + 1 Session 55 ZZZ). Pittsburgh PS + Archdiocese rows intact.

 - ✅ Diagnostic commit 68622aa reverted ( 9b51a67 → pushed 0061aed ). Railway redeploying now.

 - ✅ 5 ZZZ sentinel rows deleted.

 - ✅ Operator tooling committed ( 746e1e7 ): Telethon auth + send + recent + ZZZ cleanup scripts. I can now drive Scout end-to-end.

 Moving to Priority 1 — BUG 5 research cross-contamination. Per your instructions: enter plan mode first, ruthless pressure-test pass, then exit plan mode. Starting with code exploration so the plan is grounded

 in current reality, not memory from Session 53.

 ⏺ Entered plan mode

 Claude is now exploring and designing an implementation approach.

 ⏺ I have enough exploration grounding (the explorer agent already mapped the pipeline in detail + I've read the critical file:line ranges directly). Drafting v1 of the plan, then pressure-testing it before exit.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ Pressure-testing v1 ruthlessly.

 Weaknesses found:

 1. Over-framed L16 as the sole contamination pump. L1/L13 and any non-site:-scoped Serper layer can contaminate too. The fix is domain-agnostic and catches all of them, so the framing is misleading.

 2. Phase 2a is bloated. I'm touching contact_extractor.py in 3 ways (signature, prompt, normalization with new fields) when the validation is cleanly handled in L9.5 alone. Cut the prompt change (no measurable

 gain) and the target_district_name / target_domain new fields (unneeded, confuses schema).

 3. _domain_root via parts[0] is wrong for domains like www.nms.usd383.org — should use target_hint in source_host substring test with a min-length guard (≥5 chars).

 4. L15 interaction not addressed. L15 can ADD contacts AFTER L9.5 runs. Second L10 pass (line 161) needs to catch them — which means L10 strengthening is load-bearing, not belt-and-suspenders. The plan

 understates this.

 5. cross_contam_reattributed log column is set to 0 in v1 with no re-attribution path. Cut it.

 6. Phase 0 step 3 (verify district_domain format) is 5 minutes of reading — pollutes the plan, move to implementation notes.

 7. Verbose code skeletons in 2b/2c make the plan read like half-written code. Should be a blueprint: signatures + decision tables, not implementation sketches.

 8. No "assert known-bad rows are flagged" sanity gate in Phase 0 before running the audit on the full sheet.

 9. Drop vs re-attribute decision deserves explicit rationale beyond "DROP for v1" one-liner. Dropping the 2 ROWVA/CHSD leads hurts Steven (real leads for real districts). Should we Quarantine-tab them? Scope

 creep — but worth mentioning as the v2 follow-up.

 10. Irving ISD, TX smoke test may produce zero contacts and give an inconclusive result. Pick a known-productive target OR state the caveat.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ V2 plan written to /Users/stevenadkins/.claude/plans/abundant-finding-riddle.md . The pressure-test pass collapsed 10 weaknesses in v1 down to a leaner blueprint that:

 - Touches the extractor with only a signature extension (no prompt surgery)

 - Uses target_hint in source_host substring matching with a 5-char minimum guard (no naive parts[0] domain parsing)

 - Makes L10 strengthening load-bearing with an explicit L15-interaction note

 - Gates Phase 1 on a known-bad oracle assertion before running on the full sheet

 - Gates Phase 2 ship on a Phase 3 dry-run pass

 - Specifies Lackland ISD as the default smoke-test target with a Naperville 203 fallback

 - Keeps PII snapshot files gitignored from the start

 - Ships in 5 surgical commits

 ⎿  Tool use rejected

 ⏺ Steven ran the full 7-step pressure-test prompt. Time for a genuinely ruthless second pass. Letting me think about the whole plan before cutting.

 The biggest thing I missed in v1 and v2: I'm filtering at the wrong boundary.

 v2's validation runs AFTER extract_from_multiple — which means Claude has already been invoked on the contaminated pages, paid for the tokens, and returned contact dicts. Then we throw them away. The cheaper,

 cleaner, earlier fix is at the self.raw_pages boundary: when a page is added to the pipeline (or at the very least, after L4 discovers the target domain and before L9 extraction), filter out pages whose host

 is a clearly-different school domain. Those pages never reach Claude. The Archdiocese example would be caught here — rowva.k12.il.us and chsd218.org are unambiguously wrong-school hosts and would be dropped

 before L9 ever sees them.

 But page-level filtering alone isn't sufficient. State DOE directories, conference pages, cooperative staff pages, news articles — these are generic hosts that legitimately mention the target district

 alongside other districts. Page filter can't reject them (host is generic, benefit of the doubt). L9 extracts contacts from them. Contact-level filter then has to reject contacts whose email domain is a

 wrong-school one.

 So: two-stage filter . Page filter catches the obvious school-host-mismatch cases cheaply. Contact filter catches the subtle within-page cross-contamination on generic hosts. Both are needed, page filter

 removes ~80% of contamination before extraction cost, contact filter handles the 20% long tail.

 Other gaps from the second pass:

 - Decision rule bug in v2: source_matches OR email_matches → KEEP is wrong when source matches the target but email is from a different school (a genuinely contaminated embedded link within a legit page).

 Needs to CLEAR the email in that case.

 - GENERIC_HOSTS hardcoded set is brittle — state DOE domains vary per state. Invert: affirmative "school-like" detection is the test; everything else is treated as generic/keep.

 - No kill switch. New filters ship with ENABLE_X constants per CLAUDE.md rule. Add ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER .

 - Audit step oracle is only 2 rows thin — add a spot-check of 20 random clean_* rows to catch false negatives.

 - Sequencing: dry-run should validate the RULE before shipping the fix. Audit script uses the same rule but for historical reporting — doesn't block the ship.

 - Bookkeeping: _url_to_layer dict needs to be pruned for dropped URLs.

 - Partner/co-op district edge case (host like coopcsn12.k12.il.us that serves multiple districts) — document as known false-drop.

 ⏺ V3 written. The core architectural shift from v2: two-stage filter (page-level at raw_pages boundary BEFORE Claude extraction + contact-level on survivors) instead of single post-extraction validation. This
 catches the Archdiocese known-bad rows at stage 1 before spending Claude tokens, and lets stage 2 handle the subtler within-page contamination from generic hosts (state DOE directories, conference pages,
 etc.).

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Other high-leverage changes: shared matching helpers as single source of truth across all 4 call sites, Phase 1 dry-run as a hard gate BEFORE any live code ships, 20-row clean spot-check as a second oracle (v2
 had only 2 known-bad rows — would pass trivially), ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER kill switch, affirmative "school-like" detection instead of hardcoded generic allowlist, decision-table bug fix (source-matches
 + email-mismatches → clear email), and re-sequenced so audit runs LAST using the same validated helpers.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Full v2→v3 change list with rationale is at the bottom of the plan file.
