 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Session 55 — BUG 5: Research Pipeline Cross-Contamination Fix

 Context

 Scout's research engine writes contacts to "Leads from Research" with District Name = <research target> , but some of those contacts work at different districts. Session 53 caught this during the Archdiocese of

 Chicago smoke test: 2 of 3 "VERIFIED" leads were ROWVA CUSD 208 ( merickson@rowva.k12.il.us , scraped from rowva.k12.il.us/staff ) and CHSD 218 ( frank.lamantia@chsd218.org , scraped from chsd218.org ). Both had

 the research target stamped on them by the extractor. Steven risks sending Archdiocesan pitches to unrelated public district employees. 19 other research jobs have been logged without audit — scope is unknown.

 Where contamination enters the pipeline. Not at extraction. At self.raw_pages . Any search layer that doesn't scope by site:{district_domain} can feed contaminated pages into the extraction queue. L16 (Exa

 broad, research_engine.py:589-622 ) is the worst — query "{name} {state} CS STEM staff directory" with no domain restriction. L1/L2/L5/L13/L14/L20 also lack site scoping. By the time the pipeline reaches L9

 (Claude extraction), the contaminated pages are already in self.raw_pages and Claude will extract real contacts from them that don't belong to the target . tools/contact_extractor.py:177 then stamps

 district_name = <target> on them unconditionally.

 Why the fix has to be two-stage.

 - Stage 1 — page filter at the raw_pages boundary. Catches the obvious school-host-mismatch cases cheaply (ROWVA + CHSD both fall here). Runs BEFORE L9's Claude extraction, so contaminated pages never cost

 tokens and never produce contacts. Catches ~80% of real contamination.

 - Stage 2 — contact filter on the surviving contacts. State DOE directories, conference pages, newspaper articles — these have generic hosts and can legitimately mention the target alongside other districts.

 Page filter can't reject them. L9 extracts all contacts from them. Contact filter then rejects contacts whose email domain is a wrong-school host. Catches the 20% long tail.

 - Stage 3 — strengthened L10 with source_url check. L15 (email verification, research_engine.py:887+ ) runs after L10 and can ADD new contacts via Serper quote searches. Those additions skip stages 1 and 2

 entirely — they only pass through the second L10 call ( :161 ). Strengthening L10 with a source_url check is the only gate for L15 additions. Load-bearing, not cosmetic.

 Plus a read-only audit of existing rows so Steven knows the historical blast radius.

 Scope

 In scope.

 1. Kill switch ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER near the top of research_engine.py , default True .

 2. Stage 1 page filter: new method _filter_raw_pages_by_domain() called at the START of _layer9_claude_extraction , after L4 has populated self.district_domain .

 3. Stage 2 contact filter: new method _filter_contacts_by_domain() called immediately after self._merge_contacts(contacts) inside L9.

 4. Stage 3 L10 strengthening: add source_url hostname branch to the existing _layer10_dedup_and_score cross-district check.

 5. Shared helpers: _district_name_hint() , _is_school_host() , _host_matches_target() , _email_domain_matches_target() — single source of truth for the matching logic, reused by both filter stages, the

 strengthened L10, and the audit script.

 6. log_research_job extended with one column: cross_contam_dropped (sum of page-filter + contact-filter + L10 drops).

 7. Run dict returned by ResearchJob.run() includes pages_filtered , contacts_filtered , and cross_contam_dropped .

 8. Read-only audit script against the existing "Leads from Research" tab with a Google Doc report.

 9. Oracle dataset + dry-run harness that validate the matching rules before any live code ships.

 Out of scope.

 - Rewriting L16 / individual search layers. Filtering downstream is sufficient.

 - Bulk-rewriting historical contaminated rows. Audit surfaces them; Steven decides manual cleanup.

 - A re-attribution engine (figuring out which district a dropped contact actually works for and writing them to that district's rows).

 - BUG 1, 2, 4 (F4, F5, F8 — separate plans).

 The matching rule is the highest-leverage decision in the whole plan — it determines correctness for the page filter, the contact filter, the L10 strengthening, AND the audit fingerprinting. Validate the rule

 ONCE against a known oracle, then deploy it four places. Any plan that ships the rule to production before the oracle passes is doing it backwards.

 Order:

 1. Phase 0 — build the oracle (Archdiocese snapshot + known-bad rows + dry-run harness).

 2. Phase 1 — iterate the matching helpers against the oracle until all known-bad rows are correctly identified AND 20 random hand-picked clean rows are not. No live code touches production until this passes.

 3. Phase 2 — ship the two filter stages + L10 strengthening + log column + kill switch.

 4. Phase 3 — live smoke test via Telethon.

 5. Phase 4 — run audit script on the full historical sheet. Steven reviews the doc. Optional manual cleanup.

 Phase 4 is LAST because the audit uses the same helpers Phase 1 validated and Phase 2 deployed — we don't want to rewrite it if the rule changes mid-flight.

 Phase 0 — Oracle construction (read-only, ≤15 min)

 1. Read every row from "Leads from Research" where District Name = "Archdiocese of Chicago Schools" via service account. Write to scripts/bug5_oracle_archdiocese.json . Gitignored (PII).

 2. Read the full Prospecting Queue + 20 recently-written research log entries to pull a sample of 20 rows that are confidently clean (email domain contains the district hint, source URL host contains the

 district hint). Write to scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json . Gitignored.

 3. Hand-annotate scripts/bug5_oracle_archdiocese.json — mark the 2 known-bad rows (ROWVA + CHSD) with expected: "drop" and all others with expected: "keep" (default). This becomes the contamination oracle.

 4. Hand-annotate scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json — all rows should be expected: "keep" . This becomes the clean oracle.

 Phase 1 — Matching helpers + dry-run harness (local, no prod writes)

 Files:

 - tools/research_engine.py — new helper functions (private methods on ResearchJob ). At this phase they are NOT yet called from the live pipeline. They exist only so the dry-run harness can import them.

 - scripts/bug5_dryrun.py — new harness.

 Helpers (single source of truth for matching — same code reused in page filter, contact filter, strengthened L10, audit script):

 _district_name_hint(name: str) -> str

 lowercase + strip trailing " isd", " unified school district", " school district",

 " public schools", " city schools", " county schools", " academy",

 " catholic schools"; strip leading "archdiocese of ", "diocese of ";

 collapse whitespace. Return "" if result <5 chars (too short to be distinctive).

 _is_school_host(host: str) -> bool

 True if host contains any of: ".k12.", ".edu", "isd", "usd", "schools",

 ".ps.", "cusd", "cps", "archdiocese", "diocese", "academy", "charter"

 (case-insensitive). The affirmative test — everything else is "generic",

 which means we give benefit of the doubt and keep.

 _host_matches_target(host: str, target_host: str, target_hint: str) -> bool

 Lowercase + strip "www." from both. True if target_host is a substring of

 host, or target_hint (if ≥5 chars) is a substring of host. Empty host or

 empty target_host/target_hint → False.

 _email_domain_matches_target(email: str, target_host: str, target_hint: str) -> bool

 Parse domain from email. Same substring logic as _host_matches_target.

 Dry-run harness ( scripts/bug5_dryrun.py ):

 1. Load both oracle JSONs.

 2. Instantiate a ResearchJob("Archdiocese of Chicago Schools", "IL") .

 3. Run _layer4_district_site_search() synchronously to populate self.district_domain . If L4 doesn't return a domain, override with "archchicago.org" and log the override.

 4. For each archdiocese oracle row: construct a fake contact dict, call _filter_contacts_by_domain([contact]) , record whether it was dropped.

 5. For each clean-sample oracle row: same.

 6. Assert: every row marked expected: "drop" is dropped. Every row marked expected: "keep" is kept. Assertion failure prints which row escaped which side of the filter and exits nonzero.

 7. Also test the page-level filter: for each oracle row, synthesize a fake raw_pages entry (source_url, "fake content") and run it through _filter_raw_pages_by_domain() . Assert page-level drops match the

 subset of oracle drops where the source_url host is school-like (expect ≥ the 2 Archdiocese known-bad rows).

 Iteration: if the harness fails, the helper functions get tweaked until it passes. No other file changes. No prod writes. This is the RULE validation loop.

 Hard gate: Phase 2 does not begin until Phase 1 passes.

 Phase 2 — Ship the fix

 Five commits, one per touched file, for surgical rollback:

 Commit A — tools/research_engine.py kill switch + helpers

 Add ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER = True near SERPER_API_KEY . Add the four helper functions as ResearchJob methods (or module-level — methods preferred for self.district_domain access without passing extra

 args). No behavior change yet.

 Commit B — tools/research_engine.py Stage 1 page filter

 New method _filter_raw_pages_by_domain() . Called as the first line of _layer9_claude_extraction() BEFORE the existing two-pass content filter ( :389-414 ).

 Rule (per (url, content) tuple):

 host = parse(url).netloc, lowercase, strip "www."

 if self.district_domain and ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER:

 target_host = self.district_domain.lower().replace("www.", "")

 target_hint = self._district_name_hint(self.district_name)

 if self._host_matches_target(host, target_host, target_hint):

 KEEP # host matches target — safe

 elif self._is_school_host(host):

 DROP # host is clearly a DIFFERENT school

 else:

 KEEP # generic host (DOE, news, LinkedIn, conference) — benefit of doubt

 else:

 KEEP # no domain discovered, or kill switch off — skip filter

 On drop, also remove the URL from self._url_to_layer dict (bookkeeping cleanliness — the return dict's layer_contact_counts should reflect actual extracted pages).

 Accumulate dropped count into self._contam_pages_filtered (int).

 Log at INFO: f"L9 page filter: {dropped}/{len(before)} pages dropped as cross-district (target={target_host})" .

 Commit C — tools/research_engine.py Stage 2 contact filter + L10 strengthening

 New method _filter_contacts_by_domain() . Called immediately after self._merge_contacts(contacts) (current :440 ).

 Rule (per contact dict):

 source_url, email = contact["source_url"], contact["email"]

 host = parse(source_url).netloc, lowercase, strip "www."

 email_domain = email.split("@", 1)[1] if "@" in email else ""

 target_host, target_hint = as above

 if not (self.district_domain and ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER):

 source_matches = _host_matches_target(host, target_host, target_hint)

 email_matches = _email_domain_matches_target(email, target_host, target_hint)

 if source_matches and email_matches:

 elif source_matches and email and not email_matches and _is_school_host(email_domain):

 # Legit-target page but email links to a different school — clear the email

 contact["email"] = ""

 contact["email_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN"

 KEEP (degraded)

 elif source_matches:

 KEEP (email missing/generic/unverifiable)

 elif email_matches:

 KEEP (page host is generic/unknown, email is the authority)

 elif _is_school_host(host) and not source_matches:

 DROP (wrong-school host; any page filter miss lands here)

 elif host in ("", ) or not _is_school_host(host):

 # Generic host (DOE, news, LinkedIn). If email is missing or generic, we

 # can't judge — keep at UNKNOWN. If email is school-like but doesn't match,

 # drop (clearly extracted someone else's contact info).

 if email and _is_school_host(email_domain) and not email_matches:

 else:

 contact["email_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN"

 Accumulate dropped count into self._contam_contacts_filtered . Log at INFO.

 L10 strengthening ( _layer10_dedup_and_score , :476-489 ): add a source_url hostname branch parallel to the existing email branch. Use the same helpers. On mismatch: set email_confidence = "UNKNOWN" and increment

 self._contam_l10_cleared . DO NOT drop in L10 — L10 runs twice (once before L15, once after), and dropping would double-count and might remove L15-verified contacts. L10's job is to degrade confidence;

 dropping is the filter stages' job.

 Update ResearchJob.run() return dict ( :177-191 ):

 "pages_filtered": self._contam_pages_filtered,

 "contacts_filtered": self._contam_contacts_filtered,

 "cross_contam_dropped": self._contam_pages_filtered + self._contam_contacts_filtered,

 Initialize all three counters to 0 in __init__ .

 Commit D — tools/sheets_writer.py log schema extension

 Extend log_research_job() signature with cross_contam_dropped: int = 0 . Append the column to the Research Log tab's header constant. _ensure_tab overwrites the header on every call per root CLAUDE.md , so the

 schema migrates on first run. Old log rows will have empty trailing cells; readers must .get("cross_contam_dropped", 0) .

 Commit E — agent/main.py wiring

 In _on_research_complete , read result.get("cross_contam_dropped", 0) and pass it to log_research_job() . One-line change.

 Phase 3 — Live smoke test (via Telethon)

 Default target: Lackland ISD, TX . Justification: small (~3 min research runtime), fresh F2 queue row, has a real district domain ( lacklandisd.net ), unlikely to produce the weird diocesan/partner-school edge

 cases.

 Fallback: Naperville CUSD 203, IL if Lackland returns zero contacts.

 Steps (all driven from Claude Code via scripts/tg_send.py , scripts/tg_recent.py , and Railway log API):

 1. Fire /research Lackland ISD, TX via Telethon.

 2. Poll recent messages every ~30s (via tg_recent.py ) until Scout sends the research-complete notification.

 3. Pull Railway logs for the run window. Expect INFO lines:

 - L9 page filter: N/M pages dropped as cross-district

 - L9 contact filter: N/M contacts dropped as cross-district

 4. Read the Research Log row for Lackland ISD via service account. Confirm cross_contam_dropped column populated.

 5. Read the new "Leads from Research" rows for Lackland. Spot-check each: email domain and source_url host both contain lackland or lacklandisd .

 6. Run the audit script (Phase 4) against the new rows ONLY (filter by District Name + Date Found = today). Assert all new rows fingerprint as clean_email or clean_source .

 If any step fails: flip ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER = False via a one-line commit + push (the kill switch). Production is unblocked while the rule is fixed. Phase 1 dry-run iterates again.

 Phase 4 — Historical audit

 File: scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py .

 Uses the SAME _district_name_hint , _is_school_host , _host_matches_target , _email_domain_matches_target helpers via direct import from tools.research_engine . No duplicated logic.

 Fingerprints (mutually exclusive, checked in order):

 ┌─────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Code │ Rule │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ no_data │ Email AND Source URL both empty. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ generic │ Email is a personal-generic domain (gmail, yahoo, hotmail, outlook, icloud) AND source host isn't a school. Can't judge. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_both │ Source host and email domain both match target hint. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_source │ Source host matches; email missing or generic. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_email │ Email domain matches; source host generic or unknown. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ source_mismatch │ Source is a school-like host AND target hint not in host AND email missing/generic. Likely bad. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ email_mismatch │ Email is a school-like domain AND target hint not in email domain AND source generic. Likely bad — means L10 missed it (probably because L15 re-upgraded via Serper quote search). │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ both_mismatch │ Both of the above. Almost certainly bad. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ ambiguous │ Everything else. │

 └─────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 target_hint is derived per-row from the District Name column via _district_name_hint . No per-job canonical domain lookup — we don't have district_domain for historical rows, only District Name . This is less

 powerful than the live filter but still catches the Archdiocese case because "archdiocese of chicago schools" → hint "chicago" → substring-not-found in "rowva" or "chsd218".

 Known-bad gate: before producing the report, re-run the Phase 1 dry-run oracle assertions. If the current state of the helpers doesn't drop the 2 known-bad Archdiocese rows, the audit refuses to run and exits

 nonzero.

 Clean spot-check gate: load scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json . Assert all 20 clean-sample rows fingerprint as clean_* . If any land in source_mismatch / email_mismatch / both_mismatch , the rule has a false

 positive and the audit refuses to run.

 Output: grouped counts + per-District top 10 by contamination rate + first 5 sample rows per mismatch bucket with full data + reasoning. Written to stdout AND a Google Doc titled Research Contamination Audit —

 YYYY-MM-DD HHMM via gas_bridge.create_google_doc() . Title includes timestamp so re-runs don't collide.

 Operator review: Steven reads the doc, decides if any *_mismatch rows need manual correction or deletion. NOT part of the automated plan — human judgment.

 Critical files

 - tools/research_engine.py:382-440 — L9 entry, insertion point for both filter stages

 - tools/research_engine.py:446-522 — L10 dedup, strengthening site

 - tools/research_engine.py:109-191 — ResearchJob.run() return dict

 - tools/research_engine.py:86-107 — __init__ , where counters + helpers go

 - tools/research_engine.py:589-622 — L16 Exa broad (reference only, not modified)

 - tools/contact_extractor.py:177 — the unconditional district_name stamp (reference only, no change needed — upstream filtering makes it correct)

 - tools/sheets_writer.py:529-561 — log_research_job

 - agent/main.py:_on_research_complete — one-line wiring

 - scripts/bug5_dryrun.py — new

 - scripts/bug5_oracle_archdiocese.json — new, gitignored

 - scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json — new, gitignored

 - scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py — new

 Exit criteria

 1. Phase 1 dry-run passes: both known-bad Archdiocese rows drop, all 20 clean-sample rows keep.

 2. Phase 2 all 5 commits land on main, Railway deploys successfully.

 3. Phase 3 live smoke test on Lackland ISD: research completes, filter log lines fire, Research Log has cross_contam_dropped populated, new rows spot-check clean.

 4. Phase 4 audit script runs, known-bad + clean spot-check gates pass, Google Doc report written.

 5. Kill switch ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER present and functional (verified by temporarily setting False in dry-run harness and watching the filter no-op).

 6. 48h regression check: re-run audit against production sheet 48 hours after ship; new rows should all fingerprint clean_* .

 Risks + mitigations

 - False drops on partner / co-op / regional service agency pages (e.g. coopcsn12.k12.il.us , esu1.org — hosts that legitimately serve multiple districts). The page-level filter will drop these because the host

 doesn't contain the target hint. Mitigation: they hit the contact-level filter instead — contacts with emails at the co-op domain will fail both source and email match and get dropped. For real co-op staff who

 serve the target district, this is a false negative for lead discovery. Document as known limitation; re-attribution engine (v2) is the long-term fix.

 - self.district_domain empty after L4 fails. Filter no-ops for that run; L10's existing email check is the only defense. Not a regression.

 - Claude extraction generates contacts with wrong source_url (prompt injection, hallucination). Contact filter catches these via email domain. If both source AND email are wrong, contact lands as

 source_mismatch and drops — which is correct even if unintentional.

 - L15 additions bypass both filter stages. Caught by strengthened L10 on the second pass. Degraded to UNKNOWN, not dropped, so L15's verification work isn't wasted.

 - Target hint is <5 chars for short district names. _district_name_hint returns "" in that case; filter no-ops on hint matching and falls back to target_host only. If target_host is also missing, filter no-ops

 entirely. Safe failure mode.

 - Audit oracle is only 2 known-bad rows. Thin. Mitigated by the 20-row clean spot-check — false positives on the rule would be caught immediately. Oracle can be extended as more contaminated rows are

 discovered in production.

 - cross_contam_dropped in old log rows is empty. Readers already use .get() idiomatically; document in the commit message.

 - Smoke-test district produces zero contacts. Retry with Naperville 203, IL. If both return zero, the issue is not with the filter but with the research engine itself — separate investigation.

 - Kill switch forgotten on during a debugging session. Log line fires every run at INFO level, so its presence/absence is grep-able in Railway logs.

 - Audit Google Doc contains PII. Lives in Steven's workspace only. Never committed. Same handling as the research output docs that already exist.

 Verification plan

 Covered by exit criteria + the three hard gates (Phase 1 dry-run, Phase 3 smoke test, Phase 4 spot-check). No additional verification.

 ---

 Most important changes vs v2 (rationale)

 1. Two-stage filter instead of single post-extraction validation. v2 ran validation AFTER Claude extraction — we paid for the tokens on contaminated pages and then threw the results away. v3 filters at the

 raw_pages boundary (stage 1) BEFORE Claude sees them, then runs a contact-level filter on the survivors (stage 2) for the subtler within-page contamination. This is materially cheaper AND catches more cases.

 The ROWVA and CHSD known-bad rows both have school-host source URLs — they get dropped at stage 1 in v3 but only at stage 2 in v2, wasting extraction cost.

 2. Shared matching helpers as single source of truth. v2 had separate matching logic in the audit script, the validation layer, and the L10 strengthening. v3 has four helper functions ( _district_name_hint ,

 _is_school_host , _host_matches_target , _email_domain_matches_target ) used by all four callers. One place to change, one place to test.

 3. Phase 1 dry-run is a hard gate BEFORE ANY live code ships. v2 shipped the live code and THEN ran dry-run. v3 builds the helpers, validates them against the oracle, THEN deploys them. The matching rule is

 the highest-leverage decision in the plan; getting it right once and deploying it four places beats getting it wrong once and fixing it in four places.

 4. Clean spot-check as a second oracle. v2 had only a 2-row known-bad oracle, which would pass trivially for almost any rule. v3 adds 20 hand-picked clean rows — the rule must drop the 2 bad AND keep the 20

 good. False-positive detection is now a hard gate.

 5. ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER kill switch per the kill_switches feedback rule. v2 had no escape hatch — a bad filter would require a revert. v3 flips a bool.

 6. Affirmative "school-like" detection instead of hardcoded GENERIC_HOSTS allowlist. v2 required keeping a list of every state DOE + social network. v3 only needs to know what a school host LOOKS like;

 everything else gets benefit of doubt automatically. Simpler, more correct, lower maintenance.

 7. L10 strengthening is documented as load-bearing, not cosmetic. v2 glossed over the L15 interaction. v3 explains why the second L10 pass is the only gate for L15-added contacts, and why L10 degrades rather

 than drops (so L15 verification work isn't lost).

 8. Decision table bug fix. v2's source_matches OR email_matches → KEEP missed the case where source matches target but email is from a clearly different school. v3 adds a branch that clears the email and keeps

 the name at UNKNOWN confidence.

 9. _url_to_layer bookkeeping. v2 didn't prune the tracking dict when pages were dropped. v3 does, so the return dict's layer_contact_counts reflects actual extracted pages.

 10. Sequencing reordered: audit is LAST, not second. v2 put audit in Phase 1 with a separate iteration loop on the fingerprint rule. v3 reuses the same helpers Phase 1 validated for Phase 4's audit — one rule,

 four callers, validated once.

 11. Smoke test has an explicit kill switch fallback path. v2 said "if the smoke test fails, iterate" without specifying how production stays unblocked. v3 says: flip the kill switch, ship a one-line commit,

 production is unblocked while the rule is fixed locally.

 12. Cut the v1→v2 diff section, the "open questions" section, the Phase 5 cleanup phase, and the verbose code skeletons. v2 was 350 lines of which ~80 were meta-commentary. v3 cuts all of it. The plan is

 execution instructions, not a changelog.

 13. Out of scope is explicit and short. v2 had out-of-scope scattered across sections. v3 has one list.

 14. Commit structure is 5 clean feature commits (A kill switch + helpers, B page filter, C contact filter + L10, D log schema, E wiring). v2 bundled helpers with the filter stage.

 ---

 .env

 ⏺ Pressure-testing v1 ruthlessly.

 Weaknesses found:

 1. Over-framed L16 as the sole contamination pump. L1/L13 and any non-site:-scoped Serper layer can contaminate too. The fix is domain-agnostic and catches all of them, so the framing is misleading.

 2. Phase 2a is bloated. I'm touching contact_extractor.py in 3 ways (signature, prompt, normalization with new fields) when the validation is cleanly handled in L9.5 alone. Cut the prompt change (no measurable

 gain) and the target_district_name / target_domain new fields (unneeded, confuses schema).

 3. _domain_root via parts[0] is wrong for domains like www.nms.usd383.org — should use target_hint in source_host substring test with a min-length guard (≥5 chars).

 4. L15 interaction not addressed. L15 can ADD contacts AFTER L9.5 runs. Second L10 pass (line 161) needs to catch them — which means L10 strengthening is load-bearing, not belt-and-suspenders. The plan

 understates this.

 5. cross_contam_reattributed log column is set to 0 in v1 with no re-attribution path. Cut it.

 6. Phase 0 step 3 (verify district_domain format) is 5 minutes of reading — pollutes the plan, move to implementation notes.

 7. Verbose code skeletons in 2b/2c make the plan read like half-written code. Should be a blueprint: signatures + decision tables, not implementation sketches.

 8. No "assert known-bad rows are flagged" sanity gate in Phase 0 before running the audit on the full sheet.

 9. Drop vs re-attribute decision deserves explicit rationale beyond "DROP for v1" one-liner. Dropping the 2 ROWVA/CHSD leads hurts Steven (real leads for real districts). Should we Quarantine-tab them? Scope

 creep — but worth mentioning as the v2 follow-up.

 10. Irving ISD, TX smoke test may produce zero contacts and give an inconclusive result. Pick a known-productive target OR state the caveat.

 ⎿  Tool use rejected

 ⏺ Steven ran the full 7-step pressure-test prompt. Time for a genuinely ruthless second pass. Letting me think about the whole plan before cutting.

 The biggest thing I missed in v1 and v2: I'm filtering at the wrong boundary.

 v2's validation runs AFTER extract_from_multiple — which means Claude has already been invoked on the contaminated pages, paid for the tokens, and returned contact dicts. Then we throw them away. The cheaper,

 cleaner, earlier fix is at the self.raw_pages boundary: when a page is added to the pipeline (or at the very least, after L4 discovers the target domain and before L9 extraction), filter out pages whose host

 is a clearly-different school domain. Those pages never reach Claude. The Archdiocese example would be caught here — rowva.k12.il.us and chsd218.org are unambiguously wrong-school hosts and would be dropped

 before L9 ever sees them.

 But page-level filtering alone isn't sufficient. State DOE directories, conference pages, cooperative staff pages, news articles — these are generic hosts that legitimately mention the target district

 alongside other districts. Page filter can't reject them (host is generic, benefit of the doubt). L9 extracts contacts from them. Contact-level filter then has to reject contacts whose email domain is a

 wrong-school one.

 So: two-stage filter . Page filter catches the obvious school-host-mismatch cases cheaply. Contact filter catches the subtle within-page cross-contamination on generic hosts. Both are needed, page filter

 removes ~80% of contamination before extraction cost, contact filter handles the 20% long tail.

 Other gaps from the second pass:

 - Decision rule bug in v2: source_matches OR email_matches → KEEP is wrong when source matches the target but email is from a different school (a genuinely contaminated embedded link within a legit page).

 Needs to CLEAR the email in that case.

 - GENERIC_HOSTS hardcoded set is brittle — state DOE domains vary per state. Invert: affirmative "school-like" detection is the test; everything else is treated as generic/keep.

 - No kill switch. New filters ship with ENABLE_X constants per CLAUDE.md rule. Add ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER .

 - Audit step oracle is only 2 rows thin — add a spot-check of 20 random clean_* rows to catch false negatives.

 - Sequencing: dry-run should validate the RULE before shipping the fix. Audit script uses the same rule but for historical reporting — doesn't block the ship.

 - Bookkeeping: _url_to_layer dict needs to be pruned for dropped URLs.

 - Partner/co-op district edge case (host like coopcsn12.k12.il.us that serves multiple districts) — document as known false-drop.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ V3 written. The core architectural shift from v2: two-stage filter (page-level at raw_pages boundary BEFORE Claude extraction + contact-level on survivors) instead of single post-extraction validation. This

 catches the Archdiocese known-bad rows at stage 1 before spending Claude tokens, and lets stage 2 handle the subtler within-page contamination from generic hosts (state DOE directories, conference pages,

 etc.).

 Other high-leverage changes: shared matching helpers as single source of truth across all 4 call sites, Phase 1 dry-run as a hard gate BEFORE any live code ships, 20-row clean spot-check as a second oracle (v2

 had only 2 known-bad rows — would pass trivially), ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER kill switch, affirmative "school-like" detection instead of hardcoded generic allowlist, decision-table bug fix (source-matches

 + email-mismatches → clear email), and re-sequenced so audit runs LAST using the same validated helpers.

 Full v2→v3 change list with rationale is at the bottom of the plan file.

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan
 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/abundant-finding-riddle.md · /plan to edit
 Session 55 — BUG 5: Research Pipeline Cross-Contamination Fix

 Context

 Scout's research engine writes contacts to "Leads from Research" with District Name = <research target> , but some of those contacts work at different districts. Session 53 caught this during the Archdiocese

 of Chicago smoke test: 2 of 3 "VERIFIED" leads were ROWVA CUSD 208 ( merickson@rowva.k12.il.us , scraped from rowva.k12.il.us/staff ) and CHSD 218 ( frank.lamantia@chsd218.org , scraped from chsd218.org ). Both

 had the research target stamped on them by the extractor. Steven risks sending Archdiocesan pitches to unrelated public district employees. 19 other research jobs have been logged without audit — scope is

 unknown.

 Where contamination enters the pipeline. Not at extraction. At self.raw_pages . Any search layer that doesn't scope by site:{district_domain} can feed contaminated pages into the extraction queue. L16 (Exa

 broad, research_engine.py:589-622 ) is the worst — query "{name} {state} CS STEM staff directory" with no domain restriction. L1/L2/L5/L13/L14/L20 also lack site scoping. By the time the pipeline reaches L9

 (Claude extraction), the contaminated pages are already in self.raw_pages and Claude will extract real contacts from them that don't belong to the target . tools/contact_extractor.py:177 then stamps

 district_name = <target> on them unconditionally.

 Why the fix has to be two-stage.

 - Stage 1 — page filter at the raw_pages boundary. Catches the obvious school-host-mismatch cases cheaply (ROWVA + CHSD both fall here). Runs BEFORE L9's Claude extraction, so contaminated pages never cost

 tokens and never produce contacts. Catches ~80% of real contamination.

 - Stage 2 — contact filter on the surviving contacts. State DOE directories, conference pages, newspaper articles — these have generic hosts and can legitimately mention the target alongside other

 districts. Page filter can't reject them. L9 extracts all contacts from them. Contact filter then rejects contacts whose email domain is a wrong-school host. Catches the 20% long tail.

 - Stage 3 — strengthened L10 with source_url check. L15 (email verification, research_engine.py:887+ ) runs after L10 and can ADD new contacts via Serper quote searches. Those additions skip stages 1 and 2

 entirely — they only pass through the second L10 call ( :161 ). Strengthening L10 with a source_url check is the only gate for L15 additions. Load-bearing, not cosmetic.

 Plus a read-only audit of existing rows so Steven knows the historical blast radius.

 Scope

 In scope.

 1. Kill switch ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER near the top of research_engine.py , default True .

 2. Stage 1 page filter: new method _filter_raw_pages_by_domain() called at the START of _layer9_claude_extraction , after L4 has populated self.district_domain .

 3. Stage 2 contact filter: new method _filter_contacts_by_domain() called immediately after self._merge_contacts(contacts) inside L9.

 4. Stage 3 L10 strengthening: add source_url hostname branch to the existing _layer10_dedup_and_score cross-district check.

 5. Shared helpers: _district_name_hint() , _is_school_host() , _host_matches_target() , _email_domain_matches_target() — single source of truth for the matching logic, reused by both filter stages, the

 strengthened L10, and the audit script.

 6. log_research_job extended with one column: cross_contam_dropped (sum of page-filter + contact-filter + L10 drops).

 7. Run dict returned by ResearchJob.run() includes pages_filtered , contacts_filtered , and cross_contam_dropped .

 8. Read-only audit script against the existing "Leads from Research" tab with a Google Doc report.

 9. Oracle dataset + dry-run harness that validate the matching rules before any live code ships.

 Out of scope.

 - Rewriting L16 / individual search layers. Filtering downstream is sufficient.

 - Bulk-rewriting historical contaminated rows. Audit surfaces them; Steven decides manual cleanup.

 - A re-attribution engine (figuring out which district a dropped contact actually works for and writing them to that district's rows).

 - BUG 1, 2, 4 (F4, F5, F8 — separate plans).

 The matching rule is the highest-leverage decision in the whole plan — it determines correctness for the page filter, the contact filter, the L10 strengthening, AND the audit fingerprinting. Validate the

 rule ONCE against a known oracle, then deploy it four places. Any plan that ships the rule to production before the oracle passes is doing it backwards.

 Order:

 1. Phase 0 — build the oracle (Archdiocese snapshot + known-bad rows + dry-run harness).

 2. Phase 1 — iterate the matching helpers against the oracle until all known-bad rows are correctly identified AND 20 random hand-picked clean rows are not. No live code touches production until this

 passes.

 3. Phase 2 — ship the two filter stages + L10 strengthening + log column + kill switch.

 4. Phase 3 — live smoke test via Telethon.

 5. Phase 4 — run audit script on the full historical sheet. Steven reviews the doc. Optional manual cleanup.

 Phase 4 is LAST because the audit uses the same helpers Phase 1 validated and Phase 2 deployed — we don't want to rewrite it if the rule changes mid-flight.

 Phase 0 — Oracle construction (read-only, ≤15 min)

 1. Read every row from "Leads from Research" where District Name = "Archdiocese of Chicago Schools" via service account. Write to scripts/bug5_oracle_archdiocese.json . Gitignored (PII).

 2. Read the full Prospecting Queue + 20 recently-written research log entries to pull a sample of 20 rows that are confidently clean (email domain contains the district hint, source URL host contains the

 district hint). Write to scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json . Gitignored.

 3. Hand-annotate scripts/bug5_oracle_archdiocese.json — mark the 2 known-bad rows (ROWVA + CHSD) with expected: "drop" and all others with expected: "keep" (default). This becomes the contamination oracle.

 4. Hand-annotate scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json — all rows should be expected: "keep" . This becomes the clean oracle.

 Phase 1 — Matching helpers + dry-run harness (local, no prod writes)

 Files:

 - tools/research_engine.py — new helper functions (private methods on ResearchJob ). At this phase they are NOT yet called from the live pipeline. They exist only so the dry-run harness can import them.

 - scripts/bug5_dryrun.py — new harness.

 Helpers (single source of truth for matching — same code reused in page filter, contact filter, strengthened L10, audit script):

 _district_name_hint(name: str) -> str

 lowercase + strip trailing " isd", " unified school district", " school district",

 " public schools", " city schools", " county schools", " academy",

 " catholic schools"; strip leading "archdiocese of ", "diocese of ";

 collapse whitespace. Return "" if result <5 chars (too short to be distinctive).

 _is_school_host(host: str) -> bool

 True if host contains any of: ".k12.", ".edu", "isd", "usd", "schools",

 ".ps.", "cusd", "cps", "archdiocese", "diocese", "academy", "charter"

 (case-insensitive). The affirmative test — everything else is "generic",

 which means we give benefit of the doubt and keep.

 _host_matches_target(host: str, target_host: str, target_hint: str) -> bool

 Lowercase + strip "www." from both. True if target_host is a substring of

 host, or target_hint (if ≥5 chars) is a substring of host. Empty host or

 empty target_host/target_hint → False.

 _email_domain_matches_target(email: str, target_host: str, target_hint: str) -> bool

 Parse domain from email. Same substring logic as _host_matches_target.

 Dry-run harness ( scripts/bug5_dryrun.py ):

 1. Load both oracle JSONs.

 2. Instantiate a ResearchJob("Archdiocese of Chicago Schools", "IL") .

 3. Run _layer4_district_site_search() synchronously to populate self.district_domain . If L4 doesn't return a domain, override with "archchicago.org" and log the override.

 4. For each archdiocese oracle row: construct a fake contact dict, call _filter_contacts_by_domain([contact]) , record whether it was dropped.

 5. For each clean-sample oracle row: same.

 6. Assert: every row marked expected: "drop" is dropped. Every row marked expected: "keep" is kept. Assertion failure prints which row escaped which side of the filter and exits nonzero.

 7. Also test the page-level filter: for each oracle row, synthesize a fake raw_pages entry (source_url, "fake content") and run it through _filter_raw_pages_by_domain() . Assert page-level drops match the

 subset of oracle drops where the source_url host is school-like (expect ≥ the 2 Archdiocese known-bad rows).

 Iteration: if the harness fails, the helper functions get tweaked until it passes. No other file changes. No prod writes. This is the RULE validation loop.

 Hard gate: Phase 2 does not begin until Phase 1 passes.

 Phase 2 — Ship the fix

 Five commits, one per touched file, for surgical rollback:

 Commit A — tools/research_engine.py kill switch + helpers

 Add ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER = True near SERPER_API_KEY . Add the four helper functions as ResearchJob methods (or module-level — methods preferred for self.district_domain access without passing extra

 args). No behavior change yet.

 Commit B — tools/research_engine.py Stage 1 page filter

 New method _filter_raw_pages_by_domain() . Called as the first line of _layer9_claude_extraction() BEFORE the existing two-pass content filter ( :389-414 ).

 Rule (per (url, content) tuple):

 host = parse(url).netloc, lowercase, strip "www."

 if self.district_domain and ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER:

 target_host = self.district_domain.lower().replace("www.", "")

 target_hint = self._district_name_hint(self.district_name)

 if self._host_matches_target(host, target_host, target_hint):

 KEEP # host matches target — safe

 elif self._is_school_host(host):

 DROP # host is clearly a DIFFERENT school

 else:

 KEEP # generic host (DOE, news, LinkedIn, conference) — benefit of doubt

 else:

 KEEP # no domain discovered, or kill switch off — skip filter

 On drop, also remove the URL from self._url_to_layer dict (bookkeeping cleanliness — the return dict's layer_contact_counts should reflect actual extracted pages).

 Accumulate dropped count into self._contam_pages_filtered (int).

 Log at INFO: f"L9 page filter: {dropped}/{len(before)} pages dropped as cross-district (target={target_host})" .

 Commit C — tools/research_engine.py Stage 2 contact filter + L10 strengthening

 New method _filter_contacts_by_domain() . Called immediately after self._merge_contacts(contacts) (current :440 ).

 Rule (per contact dict):

 source_url, email = contact["source_url"], contact["email"]

 host = parse(source_url).netloc, lowercase, strip "www."

 email_domain = email.split("@", 1)[1] if "@" in email else ""

 target_host, target_hint = as above

 if not (self.district_domain and ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER):

 source_matches = _host_matches_target(host, target_host, target_hint)

 email_matches = _email_domain_matches_target(email, target_host, target_hint)

 if source_matches and email_matches:

 elif source_matches and email and not email_matches and _is_school_host(email_domain):

 # Legit-target page but email links to a different school — clear the email

 contact["email"] = ""

 contact["email_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN"

 KEEP (degraded)

 elif source_matches:

 KEEP (email missing/generic/unverifiable)

 elif email_matches:

 KEEP (page host is generic/unknown, email is the authority)

 elif _is_school_host(host) and not source_matches:

 DROP (wrong-school host; any page filter miss lands here)

 elif host in ("", ) or not _is_school_host(host):

 # Generic host (DOE, news, LinkedIn). If email is missing or generic, we

 # can't judge — keep at UNKNOWN. If email is school-like but doesn't match,

 # drop (clearly extracted someone else's contact info).

 if email and _is_school_host(email_domain) and not email_matches:

 else:

 contact["email_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN"

 Accumulate dropped count into self._contam_contacts_filtered . Log at INFO.

 L10 strengthening ( _layer10_dedup_and_score , :476-489 ): add a source_url hostname branch parallel to the existing email branch. Use the same helpers. On mismatch: set email_confidence = "UNKNOWN" and

 increment self._contam_l10_cleared . DO NOT drop in L10 — L10 runs twice (once before L15, once after), and dropping would double-count and might remove L15-verified contacts. L10's job is to degrade

 confidence; dropping is the filter stages' job.

 Update ResearchJob.run() return dict ( :177-191 ):

 "pages_filtered": self._contam_pages_filtered,

 "contacts_filtered": self._contam_contacts_filtered,

 "cross_contam_dropped": self._contam_pages_filtered + self._contam_contacts_filtered,

 Initialize all three counters to 0 in __init__ .

 Commit D — tools/sheets_writer.py log schema extension

 Extend log_research_job() signature with cross_contam_dropped: int = 0 . Append the column to the Research Log tab's header constant. _ensure_tab overwrites the header on every call per root CLAUDE.md , so

 the schema migrates on first run. Old log rows will have empty trailing cells; readers must .get("cross_contam_dropped", 0) .

 Commit E — agent/main.py wiring

 In _on_research_complete , read result.get("cross_contam_dropped", 0) and pass it to log_research_job() . One-line change.

 Phase 3 — Live smoke test (via Telethon)

 Default target: Lackland ISD, TX . Justification: small (~3 min research runtime), fresh F2 queue row, has a real district domain ( lacklandisd.net ), unlikely to produce the weird diocesan/partner-school edge

 cases.

 Fallback: Naperville CUSD 203, IL if Lackland returns zero contacts.

 Steps (all driven from Claude Code via scripts/tg_send.py , scripts/tg_recent.py , and Railway log API):

 1. Fire /research Lackland ISD, TX via Telethon.

 2. Poll recent messages every ~30s (via tg_recent.py ) until Scout sends the research-complete notification.

 3. Pull Railway logs for the run window. Expect INFO lines:

 - L9 page filter: N/M pages dropped as cross-district

 - L9 contact filter: N/M contacts dropped as cross-district

 4. Read the Research Log row for Lackland ISD via service account. Confirm cross_contam_dropped column populated.

 5. Read the new "Leads from Research" rows for Lackland. Spot-check each: email domain and source_url host both contain lackland or lacklandisd .

 6. Run the audit script (Phase 4) against the new rows ONLY (filter by District Name + Date Found = today). Assert all new rows fingerprint as clean_email or clean_source .

 If any step fails: flip ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER = False via a one-line commit + push (the kill switch). Production is unblocked while the rule is fixed. Phase 1 dry-run iterates again.

 Phase 4 — Historical audit

 File: scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py .

 Uses the SAME _district_name_hint , _is_school_host , _host_matches_target , _email_domain_matches_target helpers via direct import from tools.research_engine . No duplicated logic.

 Fingerprints (mutually exclusive, checked in order):

 ┌─────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Code │ Rule │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ no_data │ Email AND Source URL both empty. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ generic │ Email is a personal-generic domain (gmail, yahoo, hotmail, outlook, icloud) AND source host isn't a school. Can't judge. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_both │ Source host and email domain both match target hint. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_source │ Source host matches; email missing or generic. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ clean_email │ Email domain matches; source host generic or unknown. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ source_mismatch │ Source is a school-like host AND target hint not in host AND email missing/generic. Likely bad. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ email_mismatch │ Email is a school-like domain AND target hint not in email domain AND source generic. Likely bad — means L10 missed it (probably because L15 re-upgraded via Serper quote search). │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ both_mismatch │ Both of the above. Almost certainly bad. │

 ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ ambiguous │ Everything else. │

 └─────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 target_hint is derived per-row from the District Name column via _district_name_hint . No per-job canonical domain lookup — we don't have district_domain for historical rows, only District Name . This is less

 powerful than the live filter but still catches the Archdiocese case because "archdiocese of chicago schools" → hint "chicago" → substring-not-found in "rowva" or "chsd218".

 Known-bad gate: before producing the report, re-run the Phase 1 dry-run oracle assertions. If the current state of the helpers doesn't drop the 2 known-bad Archdiocese rows, the audit refuses to run and

 exits nonzero.

 Clean spot-check gate: load scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json . Assert all 20 clean-sample rows fingerprint as clean_* . If any land in source_mismatch / email_mismatch / both_mismatch , the rule has a

 false positive and the audit refuses to run.

 Output: grouped counts + per-District top 10 by contamination rate + first 5 sample rows per mismatch bucket with full data + reasoning. Written to stdout AND a Google Doc titled Research Contamination

 Audit — YYYY-MM-DD HHMM via gas_bridge.create_google_doc() . Title includes timestamp so re-runs don't collide.

 Operator review: Steven reads the doc, decides if any *_mismatch rows need manual correction or deletion. NOT part of the automated plan — human judgment.

 Critical files

 - tools/research_engine.py:382-440 — L9 entry, insertion point for both filter stages

 - tools/research_engine.py:446-522 — L10 dedup, strengthening site

 - tools/research_engine.py:109-191 — ResearchJob.run() return dict

 - tools/research_engine.py:86-107 — __init__ , where counters + helpers go

 - tools/research_engine.py:589-622 — L16 Exa broad (reference only, not modified)

 - tools/contact_extractor.py:177 — the unconditional district_name stamp (reference only, no change needed — upstream filtering makes it correct)

 - tools/sheets_writer.py:529-561 — log_research_job

 - agent/main.py:_on_research_complete — one-line wiring

 - scripts/bug5_dryrun.py — new

 - scripts/bug5_oracle_archdiocese.json — new, gitignored

 - scripts/bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json — new, gitignored

 - scripts/audit_leads_cross_contamination.py — new

 Exit criteria

 1. Phase 1 dry-run passes: both known-bad Archdiocese rows drop, all 20 clean-sample rows keep.

 2. Phase 2 all 5 commits land on main, Railway deploys successfully.

 3. Phase 3 live smoke test on Lackland ISD: research completes, filter log lines fire, Research Log has cross_contam_dropped populated, new rows spot-check clean.

 4. Phase 4 audit script runs, known-bad + clean spot-check gates pass, Google Doc report written.

 5. Kill switch ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER present and functional (verified by temporarily setting False in dry-run harness and watching the filter no-op).

 6. 48h regression check: re-run audit against production sheet 48 hours after ship; new rows should all fingerprint clean_* .

 Risks + mitigations

 - False drops on partner / co-op / regional service agency pages (e.g. coopcsn12.k12.il.us , esu1.org — hosts that legitimately serve multiple districts). The page-level filter will drop these because the

 host doesn't contain the target hint. Mitigation: they hit the contact-level filter instead — contacts with emails at the co-op domain will fail both source and email match and get dropped. For real co-op

 staff who serve the target district, this is a false negative for lead discovery. Document as known limitation; re-attribution engine (v2) is the long-term fix.

 - self.district_domain empty after L4 fails. Filter no-ops for that run; L10's existing email check is the only defense. Not a regression.

 - Claude extraction generates contacts with wrong source_url (prompt injection, hallucination). Contact filter catches these via email domain. If both source AND email are wrong, contact lands as

 source_mismatch and drops — which is correct even if unintentional.

 - L15 additions bypass both filter stages. Caught by strengthened L10 on the second pass. Degraded to UNKNOWN, not dropped, so L15's verification work isn't wasted.

 - Target hint is <5 chars for short district names. _district_name_hint returns "" in that case; filter no-ops on hint matching and falls back to target_host only. If target_host is also missing, filter

 no-ops entirely. Safe failure mode.

 - Audit oracle is only 2 known-bad rows. Thin. Mitigated by the 20-row clean spot-check — false positives on the rule would be caught immediately. Oracle can be extended as more contaminated rows are

 discovered in production.

 - cross_contam_dropped in old log rows is empty. Readers already use .get() idiomatically; document in the commit message.

 - Smoke-test district produces zero contacts. Retry with Naperville 203, IL. If both return zero, the issue is not with the filter but with the research engine itself — separate investigation.

 - Kill switch forgotten on during a debugging session. Log line fires every run at INFO level, so its presence/absence is grep-able in Railway logs.

 - Audit Google Doc contains PII. Lives in Steven's workspace only. Never committed. Same handling as the research output docs that already exist.

 Verification plan

 Covered by exit criteria + the three hard gates (Phase 1 dry-run, Phase 3 smoke test, Phase 4 spot-check). No additional verification.

 ---

 Most important changes vs v2 (rationale)

 1. Two-stage filter instead of single post-extraction validation. v2 ran validation AFTER Claude extraction — we paid for the tokens on contaminated pages and then threw the results away. v3 filters at the

 raw_pages boundary (stage 1) BEFORE Claude sees them, then runs a contact-level filter on the survivors (stage 2) for the subtler within-page contamination. This is materially cheaper AND catches more

 cases. The ROWVA and CHSD known-bad rows both have school-host source URLs — they get dropped at stage 1 in v3 but only at stage 2 in v2, wasting extraction cost.

 2. Shared matching helpers as single source of truth. v2 had separate matching logic in the audit script, the validation layer, and the L10 strengthening. v3 has four helper functions ( _district_name_hint ,

 _is_school_host , _host_matches_target , _email_domain_matches_target ) used by all four callers. One place to change, one place to test.

 3. Phase 1 dry-run is a hard gate BEFORE ANY live code ships. v2 shipped the live code and THEN ran dry-run. v3 builds the helpers, validates them against the oracle, THEN deploys them. The matching rule is

 the highest-leverage decision in the plan; getting it right once and deploying it four places beats getting it wrong once and fixing it in four places.

 4. Clean spot-check as a second oracle. v2 had only a 2-row known-bad oracle, which would pass trivially for almost any rule. v3 adds 20 hand-picked clean rows — the rule must drop the 2 bad AND keep the 20

 good. False-positive detection is now a hard gate.

 5. ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER kill switch per the kill_switches feedback rule. v2 had no escape hatch — a bad filter would require a revert. v3 flips a bool.

 6. Affirmative "school-like" detection instead of hardcoded GENERIC_HOSTS allowlist. v2 required keeping a list of every state DOE + social network. v3 only needs to know what a school host LOOKS like;

 everything else gets benefit of doubt automatically. Simpler, more correct, lower maintenance.

 7. L10 strengthening is documented as load-bearing, not cosmetic. v2 glossed over the L15 interaction. v3 explains why the second L10 pass is the only gate for L15-added contacts, and why L10 degrades

 rather than drops (so L15 verification work isn't lost).

 8. Decision table bug fix. v2's source_matches OR email_matches → KEEP missed the case where source matches target but email is from a clearly different school. v3 adds a branch that clears the email and

 keeps the name at UNKNOWN confidence.

 9. _url_to_layer bookkeeping. v2 didn't prune the tracking dict when pages were dropped. v3 does, so the return dict's layer_contact_counts reflects actual extracted pages.

 10. Sequencing reordered: audit is LAST, not second. v2 put audit in Phase 1 with a separate iteration loop on the fingerprint rule. v3 reuses the same helpers Phase 1 validated for Phase 4's audit — one

 rule, four callers, validated once.

 11. Smoke test has an explicit kill switch fallback path. v2 said "if the smoke test fails, iterate" without specifying how production stays unblocked. v3 says: flip the kill switch, ship a one-line commit,

 production is unblocked while the rule is fixed locally.

 12. Cut the v1→v2 diff section, the "open questions" section, the Phase 5 cleanup phase, and the verbose code skeletons. v2 was 350 lines of which ~80 were meta-commentary. v3 cuts all of it. The plan is

 execution instructions, not a changelog.

 13. Out of scope is explicit and short. v2 had out-of-scope scattered across sections. v3 has one list.

 14. Commit structure is 5 clean feature commits (A kill switch + helpers, B page filter, C contact filter + L10, D log schema, E wiring). v2 bundled helpers with the filter stage.

 ⏺ Pressure-testing v1 ruthlessly.

 Weaknesses found:

 1. Over-framed L16 as the sole contamination pump. L1/L13 and any non-site:-scoped Serper layer can contaminate too. The fix is domain-agnostic and catches all of them, so the framing is misleading.

 2. Phase 2a is bloated. I'm touching contact_extractor.py in 3 ways (signature, prompt, normalization with new fields) when the validation is cleanly handled in L9.5 alone. Cut the prompt change (no measurable

 gain) and the target_district_name / target_domain new fields (unneeded, confuses schema).

 3. _domain_root via parts[0] is wrong for domains like www.nms.usd383.org — should use target_hint in source_host substring test with a min-length guard (≥5 chars).

 4. L15 interaction not addressed. L15 can ADD contacts AFTER L9.5 runs. Second L10 pass (line 161) needs to catch them — which means L10 strengthening is load-bearing, not belt-and-suspenders. The plan

 understates this.

 5. cross_contam_reattributed log column is set to 0 in v1 with no re-attribution path. Cut it.

 6. Phase 0 step 3 (verify district_domain format) is 5 minutes of reading — pollutes the plan, move to implementation notes.

 7. Verbose code skeletons in 2b/2c make the plan read like half-written code. Should be a blueprint: signatures + decision tables, not implementation sketches.

 8. No "assert known-bad rows are flagged" sanity gate in Phase 0 before running the audit on the full sheet.

 9. Drop vs re-attribute decision deserves explicit rationale beyond "DROP for v1" one-liner. Dropping the 2 ROWVA/CHSD leads hurts Steven (real leads for real districts). Should we Quarantine-tab them? Scope

 creep — but worth mentioning as the v2 follow-up.

 10. Irving ISD, TX smoke test may produce zero contacts and give an inconclusive result. Pick a known-productive target OR state the caveat.

 ⏺ V2 plan written to /Users/stevenadkins/.claude/plans/abundant-finding-riddle.md . The pressure-test pass collapsed 10 weaknesses in v1 down to a leaner blueprint that:

 - Touches the extractor with only a signature extension (no prompt surgery)

 - Uses target_hint in source_host substring matching with a 5-char minimum guard (no naive parts[0] domain parsing)

 - Makes L10 strengthening load-bearing with an explicit L15-interaction note

 - Gates Phase 1 on a known-bad oracle assertion before running on the full sheet

 - Gates Phase 2 ship on a Phase 3 dry-run pass

 - Specifies Lackland ISD as the default smoke-test target with a Naperville 203 fallback
