 Research Log headers (8): ['Date', 'District', 'State', 'Layers Used', 'Total Found', 'With Email', 'No Email', 'Notes']

 Latest Lackland Research Log row:

 Date: 2026-04-10 23:23

 District: Lackland ISD

 State: TX

 Layers Used: L1:direct-title, L2:title-variations, L3:linkedin, L5:news-grants, L16:exa-broad, L20:brave, L4:district-site, L6:scrape, L11:school-staff, L12:board-agendas, L13:state-doe, L14:conference,

 L17:exa-domain, L7:deep-crawl, L8:email-inference, L9:claude-extract, L10:dedup-score, L15:email-verify, L10:dedup-score

 … +12 lines ( ctrl+o to see all)

 ⏺ Two issues found:

 1. Research Log schema didn't migrate — the new Cross-Contam Dropped column is missing from the header. ensure_sheet_tabs_exist() must not actually overwrite existing headers.

 2. One contaminated row slipped through Stage 2 : Phil Voight @centralislip.k12.ny.us from tips-usa.com. Central Islip NY is not Lackland TX, and the email clearly matches my "generic source + wrong-school

 email → DROP" rule. Need to debug why it wasn't caught.

 ⏺ Two real bugs:

 1. _ensure_headers only writes if the tab is empty — it's NOT the auto-migrating function I assumed. Need to extend it: if current header is a prefix of expected columns, migrate forward.

 2. L15-added contact slipped through. L15 runs after Stage 2, so its additions only have L10 as a gate. L10's existing email check uses domain_root = email_domain.split(".")[0] = "centralislip" which doesn't

 contain any of isd/usd/k12/etc . Fix: L10 should use my new helpers ( _is_school_host on the full hostname) so centralislip.k12.ny.us is correctly flagged.

 ⏺ Found the real root cause. L15 calls _merge_contacts(new_contacts) directly at two sites (lines 1341, 1376) — it never goes through Stage 2 . The right fix is to call Stage 2 after each L15 merge, so L15

 additions are filtered the same way L9 additions are. This is cleaner than fattening L10.

 1 #!/usr/bin/env python3

 2 """Delete all Lackland ISD rows from today's test runs so the re-smoke-test

 3 has a clean slate. """

 4 import os, json

 5 from pathlib import Path

 7 for line in (Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / ".env" ).read_text().splitlines():

 8 line = line.strip()

 9 if not line or line.startswith( "#" ) or "=" not in line: continue

 10 k, _, v = line.partition( "=" )

 733 continue

 736 + # Cross-district email validation .

 737 + # BUG 5 Session 55: rewritten to use the shared helpers so the

 738 + # whole hostname is inspected (not just parts[0]). The old

 739 + # implementation missed emails like "@centralislip.k12.ny.us"

 740 + # because parts[0] = "centralislip" contains no district pattern.

 741 + if email and "@" in email and domain and ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER:

 743 + target_host_l10 = self.district_domain.lower().replace( "www." , "" ) if self.district_domain else ""

 746 + email_domain

 751 + ):

 753 + c[ "email" ] = ""

 754 + c[ "email_confidence" ] = "UNKNOWN"

 755 + rejected[ "cross_district" ] += 1

 757 # BUG 5: L10 source_url strengthening. Catches L15-added contacts

 758 # that bypass both filter stages. Degrades confidence to UNKNOWN;

 ⏺ Found the real root cause. L15 calls _merge_contacts(new_contacts) directly at two sites (lines 1341, 1376) — it never goes through Stage 2 . The right fix is to call Stage 2 after each L15 merge, so L15

 additions are filtered the same way L9 additions are. This is cleaner than fattening L10.

 ⏺ Found the real root cause. L15 calls _merge_contacts(new_contacts) directly at two sites (lines 1341, 1376) — it never goes through Stage 2 . The right fix is to call Stage 2 after each L15 merge, so L15

 additions are filtered the same way L9 additions are. This is cleaner than fattening L10.

 ⏺ Update ( tools/research_engine.py )

 ⎿   Added 3 lines

 1339 None , extract_from_multiple, enrichment_raw, self.district_name

 1340 )

 1341 self._merge_contacts(new_contacts)

 1342 + # BUG 5: run Stage 2 over L15 additions so they get the

 1343 + # same cross-district filtering L9 additions do

 1344 + self._filter_contacts_by_domain()

 1346 await asyncio.sleep( 1.0 )

 ⏺ Update ( tools/research_engine.py )

 ⎿   Added 2 lines

 1377 None , extract_from_multiple, discovery_raw, self.district_name

 1378 )

 1379 self._merge_contacts(new_contacts)

 1380 + # BUG 5: run Stage 2 over L15 discovery additions too

 1381 + self._filter_contacts_by_domain()

 1383 logger.info(

 1384 f"L15 complete for {self.district_name} : {_l15_used} queries used, "

 ⏺ One more fix — _contam_contacts_filtered = dropped uses = which means the second call from L15 overwrites the first call's count from L9. Needs to be += .

 ⏺ Update ( tools/research_engine.py )

 ⎿   Added 1 line, removed 1 line

 692 kept.append(c)

 694 self.all_contacts = kept

 695 - self._contam_contacts_filtered = dropped

 695 + self._contam_contacts_filtered + = dropped

 697 logger.info(

 698 f"L9 contact filter: {dropped} / {before} contacts dropped as cross-district, "

 Important nuance in the flagged rows — some are false positives due to district-name-vs-abbreviation mismatch (which only affects the audit, NOT the live filter):

 fix-research-pipeline-contamination

 - Los Angeles Unified : 9 flagged rows, all @lausd.net. These are legitimately LA Unified staff — the audit fails because target_hint = "losangelesunified" isn't a substring of "lausd.net" . The LIVE filter in
 production would NOT false-positive because it uses self.district_domain = "lausd.net" (discovered by L4). Historical rows don't have that stored.

 Important nuance in the flagged rows — some are false positives due to district-name-vs-abbreviation mismatch (which only affects the audit, NOT the live filter):

 - Los Angeles Unified : 9 flagged rows, all @lausd.net. These are legitimately LA Unified staff — the audit fails because target_hint = "losangelesunified" isn't a substring of "lausd.net" . The LIVE filter in

 production would NOT false-positive because it uses self.district_domain = "lausd.net" (discovered by L4). Historical rows don't have that stored.

 - Desert Sands Unified row 212: dsusd.us — legit, same abbreviation problem.

 Real contamination worth manual review:

 - Archdiocese of Chicago rows 458, 459 (the original Session 53 finding — confirmed)

 - Epic Charter rows 216, 217: Melissa Carder @collinsville.k12.ok.us, K. Craig @spiro.k12.ok.us — unambiguously wrong employers (Collinsville and Spiro are unrelated Oklahoma districts)

 - Columbus City Schools row 333: cisenhour@wscloud.org, source from Worthington k12.oh.us — Worthington is a different Ohio district

 - Friendswood ISD row 15: thanks@fisdk12.net — could be legit (fisd = Friendswood ISD), needs manual check

 - Irving STEAM Magnet rows: 2 flagged, need to inspect

 Google Doc report : https://docs.google.com/document/d/1TFle1jiy EiFqU_hv-rx Ixs Cf-WxXXDRo RaKW2A6MEfA/edit

 Important nuance in the flagged rows — some are false positives due to district-name-vs-abbreviation mismatch (which only affects the audit, NOT the live filter):

 - Los Angeles Unified : 9 flagged rows, all @lausd.net. These are legitimately LA Unified staff — the audit fails because target_hint = "losangelesunified" isn't a substring of "lausd.net" . The LIVE filter in

 production would NOT false-positive because it uses self.district_domain = "lausd.net" (discovered by L4). Historical rows don't have that stored.

 - Desert Sands Unified row 212: dsusd.us — legit, same abbreviation problem.

 Real contamination worth manual review:

 - Archdiocese of Chicago rows 458, 459 (the original Session 53 finding — confirmed)

 - Epic Charter rows 216, 217: Melissa Carder @collinsville.k12.ok.us, K. Craig @spiro.k12.ok.us — unambiguously wrong employers (Collinsville and Spiro are unrelated Oklahoma districts)

 - Columbus City Schools row 333: cisenhour@wscloud.org, source from Worthington k12.oh.us — Worthington is a different Ohio district

 - Friendswood ISD row 15: thanks@fisdk12.net — could be legit (fisd = Friendswood ISD), needs manual check

 - Irving STEAM Magnet rows: 2 flagged, need to inspect

 47 - ### Session 54 lessons (bank these into future sessions )

 48 - - **Snapshot before any destructive repair.** `scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py` creates a backup tab via `duplicateSheet` API call BEFORE clearing. Non-negotiable. Every future one-shot r

 - epair script should inherit this pattern.

 49 - - **Dry-run-then-apply is a hard flag-gate, not a casual print.** The repair script's `--dry-run` is default, `--apply` is opt-in, and the operator reviews the diff before flipping.

 50 - - **Verify before asking.** When you're about to ask Steven a question, first try to answer it yourself via env check, config read, git log, tool query, memory lookup. Only ask if the tool resul

 - t is ambiguous, the answer is a preference Steven alone can give, or picking wrong has high cost. Saved as `memory/feedback_verify_before_asking.md`.

 51 - - **The ghost writer hunt ended up less important than the repair.** Four independent sentinel tests reproduced canonical rows, proving the code IS correct when called directly. The investigatio

 - n burned hours. Lesson: once the REPAIR path is viable, don't keep drilling on the root cause — ship the repair, add diagnostic logging that will catch the next occurrence, move on.

 52 - - **Railway rolling deploys remain a temporal risk.** Session 53's hypothesis about rolling deploy overlap was partially right — 20+ deployments in one day create windows where old and new pods

 - could theoretically overlap. Not root cause for tonight's BUG 3 but keep in mind for future intermittent weirdness.

 53 - - **Strategy-dispatch readers beat universal content-based recovery** for multi-template scrambles. Each scanner's row-construction order is deterministic per strategy, so reading fields from kn

 - own positions is more robust than heuristic content classification.

 47 + ### Session 55 lessons (bank these)

 48 + - **Filter at the earliest boundary that has the signal, not at the output.** BUG 5 plan v2 filtered after Claude extraction (wasted tokens). v3 filters at `raw_pages` boundary before Claude see

 + s the pages. Saved as `memory/feedback_filter_upstream_not_downstream.md`. Walk the pipeline backwards in plan mode and find the earliest stage where the needed signal first becomes available.

 49 + - **Don't ask permission on verified capabilities.** Steven: "you dont need to ask to do that you should have just done it" (re: testing screencapture). Asking on safe reversible known-working a

 + ctions burns latency. Saved as `memory/feedback_take_initiative_on_verified_capabilities.md`. Still ask on destructive ops, ambiguous decisions, or money-spending.

 50 + - **Shared helpers as single source of truth.** BUG 5 has 4 callers of the same matching logic (Stage 1, Stage 2, strengthened L10, audit script). One helper, four callers. DRY prevents the "4 p

 + laces with subtly different rules" failure mode.

 51 + - **Known-bad oracle is necessary but not sufficient.** 2-row oracle passes trivially for almost any rule. Add a 20-row "must be kept" clean spot-check as a second gate. Both gates block the aud

 + it script from producing a report until they pass.

 52 + - **L15 additions bypass anything that runs in L9.** Any post-L9 validation must ALSO run on L15's `_merge_contacts` sites (two of them). The second L10 pass is a weaker gate than a Stage 2 call

 + .

 53 + - **`sheets_writer._ensure_headers` was NOT the auto-migrating function I assumed from root CLAUDE.md.** Root CLAUDE.md's "`_ensure_tab` always overwrites" refers to a different function in `dis

 + trict_prospector.py`. Now fixed: `_ensure_headers` auto-migrates when current header is a prefix of expected.

 54 + - **Railway log API queries need Date Time scalar type** (not String). `start Date: Date Time` / `end Date: Date Time`. 5000-line limit per query — narrow the window if near the cap.

 55 + - **Scout bot research task can occasionally hang silently for 1 request then recover.** Session 55 second smoke test hung for 20+ min with zero progress logs, then a third attempt completed in

 + 7 minutes. Possibly a 409 Conflict overlap during rolling deploy. If research seems stuck, wait 2-3 min and retry; don't assume a code bug first.

 55 - ### Session 54 lessons to carry forward — from Session 53

 56 - - **Spot-check EVERY scanner output via direct sheet read, not Telegram message.** Telegram reported "F2 auto-queued 8" which sounded like a win in Session 53. Direct sheet read showed 8 rows at

 - wrong column positions. From Session 54 onward, "spot-check" means "read the actual sheet row via service account" not "trust the Telegram ack."

 57 - - **"Not a regression" is not the same as "not a bug".** F4 has been returning 0 since Session 49, nobody noticed. BUG 3 was happening for WEEKS before Session 53 caught it. Break the habituatio

 - n by periodically direct-reading sheet state.

 58 - - **Kill switches need to be used.** Session 54 used them correctly (F2/F8 disabled during investigation, re-enabled after fix). Keep that discipline.

 59 -

 auto-queue). Daily 7:45 AM + weekly Monday + monthly 1st Monday schedule unchanged.

 ❯ We're approaching context limits. Before I clear, do the following in order:

 1. Save any feedback, preferences, or user context learned this session to memory

 2. Update the project plan file (e.g. SCOUT_PLAN.md):

 - Mark completed items as done

 - Add any new tasks or additions that came up this session

 - Update 'Current Focus' to reflect exactly where we are

 - If we're mid-task, note precisely what was being attempted and what state it's in

 3. Maintain CLAUDE.md:

 - Add any new rules, conventions, or architectural decisions from this session

 - Update 'Current State' with: what's working, what's broken or in-progress, and the exact next step to take

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 47 - ### Session 54 lessons (bank these into future sessions )

 48 - - **Snapshot before any destructive repair.** `scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py` creates a backup tab via `duplicateSheet` API call BEFORE clearing. Non-negotiable. Every future one-shot r

 - epair script should inherit this pattern.

 49 - - **Dry-run-then-apply is a hard flag-gate, not a casual print.** The repair script's `--dry-run` is default, `--apply` is opt-in, and the operator reviews the diff before flipping.

 50 - - **Verify before asking.** When you're about to ask Steven a question, first try to answer it yourself via env check, config read, git log, tool query, memory lookup. Only ask if the tool resul

 - t is ambiguous, the answer is a preference Steven alone can give, or picking wrong has high cost. Saved as `memory/feedback_verify_before_asking.md`.

 51 - - **The ghost writer hunt ended up less important than the repair.** Four independent sentinel tests reproduced canonical rows, proving the code IS correct when called directly. The investigatio

 - n burned hours. Lesson: once the REPAIR path is viable, don't keep drilling on the root cause — ship the repair, add diagnostic logging that will catch the next occurrence, move on.

 52 - - **Railway rolling deploys remain a temporal risk.** Session 53's hypothesis about rolling deploy overlap was partially right — 20+ deployments in one day create windows where old and new pods

 - could theoretically overlap. Not root cause for tonight's BUG 3 but keep in mind for future intermittent weirdness.

 53 - - **Strategy-dispatch readers beat universal content-based recovery** for multi-template scrambles. Each scanner's row-construction order is deterministic per strategy, so reading fields from kn

 - own positions is more robust than heuristic content classification.

 47 + ### Session 55 lessons (bank these)

 48 + - **Filter at the earliest boundary that has the signal, not at the output.** BUG 5 plan v2 filtered after Claude extraction (wasted tokens). v3 filters at `raw_pages` boundary before Claude see

 + s the pages. Saved as `memory/feedback_filter_upstream_not_downstream.md`. Walk the pipeline backwards in plan mode and find the earliest stage where the needed signal first becomes available.

 49 + - **Don't ask permission on verified capabilities.** Steven: "you dont need to ask to do that you should have just done it" (re: testing screencapture). Asking on safe reversible known-working a

 + ctions burns latency. Saved as `memory/feedback_take_initiative_on_verified_capabilities.md`. Still ask on destructive ops, ambiguous decisions, or money-spending.

 50 + - **Shared helpers as single source of truth.** BUG 5 has 4 callers of the same matching logic (Stage 1, Stage 2, strengthened L10, audit script). One helper, four callers. DRY prevents the "4 p

 + laces with subtly different rules" failure mode.

 51 + - **Known-bad oracle is necessary but not sufficient.** 2-row oracle passes trivially for almost any rule. Add a 20-row "must be kept" clean spot-check as a second gate. Both gates block the aud

 + it script from producing a report until they pass.

 52 + - **L15 additions bypass anything that runs in L9.** Any post-L9 validation must ALSO run on L15's `_merge_contacts` sites (two of them). The second L10 pass is a weaker gate than a Stage 2 call

 + .

 53 + - **`sheets_writer._ensure_headers` was NOT the auto-migrating function I assumed from root CLAUDE.md.** Root CLAUDE.md's "`_ensure_tab` always overwrites" refers to a different function in `dis

 + trict_prospector.py`. Now fixed: `_ensure_headers` auto-migrates when current header is a prefix of expected.

 54 + - **Railway log API queries need Date Time scalar type** (not String). `start Date: Date Time` / `end Date: Date Time`. 5000-line limit per query — narrow the window if near the cap.

 55 + - **Scout bot research task can occasionally hang silently for 1 request then recover.** Session 55 second smoke test hung for 20+ min with zero progress logs, then a third attempt completed in

 + 7 minutes. Possibly a 409 Conflict overlap during rolling deploy. If research seems stuck, wait 2-3 min and retry; don't assume a code bug first.

 55 - ### Session 54 lessons to carry forward — from Session 53

 56 - - **Spot-check EVERY scanner output via direct sheet read, not Telegram message.** Telegram reported "F2 auto-queued 8" which sounded like a win in Session 53. Direct sheet read showed 8 rows at

 - wrong column positions. From Session 54 onward, "spot-check" means "read the actual sheet row via service account" not "trust the Telegram ack."

 57 - - **"Not a regression" is not the same as "not a bug".** F4 has been returning 0 since Session 49, nobody noticed. BUG 3 was happening for WEEKS before Session 53 caught it. Break the habituatio

 - n by periodically direct-reading sheet state.

 58 - - **Kill switches need to be used.** Session 54 used them correctly (F2/F8 disabled during investigation, re-enabled after fix). Keep that discipline.

 59 -

 59 - Signal Intelligence System: ✅ — **22 sources**. **40+ Telegram commands**. Session 52 added `/reprioritize_pending` (one-shot migration). F9 compliance scanner is now Signals-only pilot (not

 auto-queue). Daily 7:45 AM + weekly Monday + monthly 1st Monday schedule unchanged.

 ⏺ Update ( SCOUT_PLAN.md )

 ⎿   Added 59 lines, removed 2 lines

 1 # SCOUT MASTER PLAN

 2 - *Last updated: 2026-04- 10 — End of Session 54 (BUG 3 repair + writer fixes )*

 2 + *Last updated: 2026-04- 11 — End of Session 55 (BUG 3 sentinel close-out + BUG 5 fix )*

 4 ---

 6 - ## YOU ARE HERE → Session 54 shipped BUG 3 end-to-end. 1952 scrambled rows in Prospecting Queue rebuilt in canonical layout, 5 writer bugs patched, 2 latent bugs fixed, backup tab preserved. Gho

 - st writer root cause still unidentified (diagnostic logging deployed to capture on next F2 run). **Next up: BUG 5 (research cross-contamination) — Priority 2 in the sprint order.** 4 bugs remain

 - before the Session 52 Stage 6-8 carryover can run.

 6 + ## YOU ARE HERE → Session 55 closed BUG 3 end-to-end AND shipped BUG 5 (research cross-contamination). Priority 0: captured live production `[BUG3_DIAG]` evidence via Telethon-driven sentinel, c

 + onfirmed canonical 20-col write (`A1960:T1960 pos_of_strategy=[8]`), reverted diagnostic logging, deleted 5 ZZZ sentinel rows. Priority 1: two-stage cross-district filter shipped (Stage 1 page f

 + ilter at raw_pages boundary + Stage 2 contact filter + strengthened L10 + schema migration). Live smoke test on Lackland ISD: 9/234 pages dropped, 25/25 new rows fingerprint clean. Historical au

 + dit on 483 rows: 95% clean, 4.8% flagged for Steven's manual review. Also built Telethon bridge for end-to-end Scout automation from Claude Code. **Next up: BUG 4 (F8 diocesan playbook) — Priori

 + ty 2.** 3 bugs remain before Session 52 Stage 6-8 carryover.

 8 + ### Session 55 deliverables

 9 +

 10 + **Priority 0 — BUG 3 Phase 6 sentinel close-out:**

 11 + - Pulled Railway logs for the 12:45 UTC scheduled scan window — discovered F2 is NOT in the daily scheduled scan (only `/signal_competitors` manual trigger). Session 54's "scheduled 7:45 AM CDT"

 + assumption was wrong.

 12 + - Built Telethon bridge: `scripts/telethon_auth.py`, `scripts/tg_send.py`, `scripts/tg_recent.py`. One-time phone auth completed. Session file at `.telethon_session` (gitignored).

 13 + - Fired `/prospect_add ZZZ_SESSION55_SENTINEL_DELETEME, TX` via Telethon through live Railway bot. Diagnostic log captured:

 14 + ```

 15 + [BUG3_DIAG] _write_rows row len=20 first3=['TX','ZZZ_SESSION55_SENTINEL_DELETEME',''] pos_of_strategy=[8]

 16 + updated Range='Prospecting Queue'!A1960:T1960 updated Rows=1 updated Cells=20

 17 + ```

 18 + Canonical 20-col write via normal `add_district → _write_rows` path. BUG 3 confirmed dead.

 19 + - Reverted commit `68622aa` via `9b51a67` → pushed `0061aed`.

 20 + - Deleted 5 ZZZ sentinel rows (1956-1960).

 21 + - Committed Telethon infrastructure as `746e1e7`.

 22 +

 23 + **Priority 1 — BUG 5 two-stage cross-district contamination filter:**

 24 + - Entered plan mode. Wrote v1, self-pressure-tested, wrote v2, Steven ran the 7-step ruthless pressure-test prompt, wrote v3 from scratch. Biggest architectural shift across passes: filter at `r

 + aw_pages` boundary BEFORE Claude extraction, not after (cheaper AND more effective).

 25 + - **Phase 0 oracle construction:** `scripts/bug5_phase0_scan.py` built `bug5_oracle_archdiocese.json` (3 rows, 2 known-bad marked) and `bug5_oracle_clean_sample.json` (20 rows across 9 districts

 + ). Both gitignored.

 26 + - **Phase 1 dry-run hard gate:** `scripts/bug5_dryrun.py` validates matching helpers against both oracles. 23/23 rows classify correctly before any live code ships.

 27 + - **Phase 2 — 6 commits landed on main:**

 28 + - `6ffa1b2` Commit A: `ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER` kill switch + 4 shared matching helpers (`_district_name_hint`, `_is_school_host`, `_host_matches_target`, `_email_domain_matches_target`)

 + + 3 counters

 29 + - `552240f` Commit B: `_filter_raw_pages_by_domain()` — Stage 1 page filter, called at start of `_layer9_claude_extraction`. Drops pages whose host is a different school BEFORE Claude extracti

 + on.

 30 + - `148aca6` Commit C: `_filter_contacts_by_domain()` — Stage 2 contact filter, called after `_merge_contacts`. Strengthened L10 with source_url hostname branch + rewrote L10 email check to use

 + new helpers (old `parts[0]` domain parsing missed real school domains).

 31 + - `4bdfcfc` Commit D: `sheets_writer.log_research_job()` extended with `cross_contam_dropped` kwarg + new "Cross-Contam Dropped" column

 47 - ### Session 54 lessons (bank these into future sessions )

 48 - - **Snapshot before any destructive repair.** `scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py` creates a backup tab via `duplicateSheet` API call BEFORE clearing. Non-negotiable. Every future one-shot r

 - epair script should inherit this pattern.

 49 - - **Dry-run-then-apply is a hard flag-gate, not a casual print.** The repair script's `--dry-run` is default, `--apply` is opt-in, and the operator reviews the diff before flipping.

 50 - - **Verify before asking.** When you're about to ask Steven a question, first try to answer it yourself via env check, config read, git log, tool query, memory lookup. Only ask if the tool resul

 - t is ambiguous, the answer is a preference Steven alone can give, or picking wrong has high cost. Saved as `memory/feedback_verify_before_asking.md`.

 51 - - **The ghost writer hunt ended up less important than the repair.** Four independent sentinel tests reproduced canonical rows, proving the code IS correct when called directly. The investigatio

 - n burned hours. Lesson: once the REPAIR path is viable, don't keep drilling on the root cause — ship the repair, add diagnostic logging that will catch the next occurrence, move on.

 52 - - **Railway rolling deploys remain a temporal risk.** Session 53's hypothesis about rolling deploy overlap was partially right — 20+ deployments in one day create windows where old and new pods

 - could theoretically overlap. Not root cause for tonight's BUG 3 but keep in mind for future intermittent weirdness.

 53 - - **Strategy-dispatch readers beat universal content-based recovery** for multi-template scrambles. Each scanner's row-construction order is deterministic per strategy, so reading fields from kn

 - own positions is more robust than heuristic content classification.

 47 + ### Session 55 lessons (bank these)

 48 + - **Filter at the earliest boundary that has the signal, not at the output.** BUG 5 plan v2 filtered after Claude extraction (wasted tokens). v3 filters at `raw_pages` boundary before Claude see

 + s the pages. Saved as `memory/feedback_filter_upstream_not_downstream.md`. Walk the pipeline backwards in plan mode and find the earliest stage where the needed signal first becomes available.

 49 + - **Don't ask permission on verified capabilities.** Steven: "you dont need to ask to do that you should have just done it" (re: testing screencapture). Asking on safe reversible known-working a

 + ctions burns latency. Saved as `memory/feedback_take_initiative_on_verified_capabilities.md`. Still ask on destructive ops, ambiguous decisions, or money-spending.

 50 + - **Shared helpers as single source of truth.** BUG 5 has 4 callers of the same matching logic (Stage 1, Stage 2, strengthened L10, audit script). One helper, four callers. DRY prevents the "4 p

 + laces with subtly different rules" failure mode.

 51 + - **Known-bad oracle is necessary but not sufficient.** 2-row oracle passes trivially for almost any rule. Add a 20-row "must be kept" clean spot-check as a second gate. Both gates block the aud

 + it script from producing a report until they pass.

 52 + - **L15 additions bypass anything that runs in L9.** Any post-L9 validation must ALSO run on L15's `_merge_contacts` sites (two of them). The second L10 pass is a weaker gate than a Stage 2 call

 + .

 53 + - **`sheets_writer._ensure_headers` was NOT the auto-migrating function I assumed from root CLAUDE.md.** Root CLAUDE.md's "`_ensure_tab` always overwrites" refers to a different function in `dis

 + trict_prospector.py`. Now fixed: `_ensure_headers` auto-migrates when current header is a prefix of expected.

 54 + - **Railway log API queries need Date Time scalar type** (not String). `start Date: Date Time` / `end Date: Date Time`. 5000-line limit per query — narrow the window if near the cap.

 55 + - **Scout bot research task can occasionally hang silently for 1 request then recover.** Session 55 second smoke test hung for 20+ min with zero progress logs, then a third attempt completed in

 + 7 minutes. Possibly a 409 Conflict overlap during rolling deploy. If research seems stuck, wait 2-3 min and retry; don't assume a code bug first.

 55 - ### Session 54 lessons to carry forward — from Session 53

 56 - - **Spot-check EVERY scanner output via direct sheet read, not Telegram message.** Telegram reported "F2 auto-queued 8" which sounded like a win in Session 53. Direct sheet read showed 8 rows at

 - wrong column positions. From Session 54 onward, "spot-check" means "read the actual sheet row via service account" not "trust the Telegram ack."

 57 - - **"Not a regression" is not the same as "not a bug".** F4 has been returning 0 since Session 49, nobody noticed. BUG 3 was happening for WEEKS before Session 53 caught it. Break the habituatio

 - n by periodically direct-reading sheet state.

 58 - - **Kill switches need to be used.** Session 54 used them correctly (F2/F8 disabled during investigation, re-enabled after fix). Keep that discipline.

 59 -

 59 - Signal Intelligence System: ✅ — **22 sources**. **40+ Telegram commands**. Session 52 added `/reprioritize_pending` (one-shot migration). F9 compliance scanner is now Signals-only pilot (not

 auto-queue). Daily 7:45 AM + weekly Monday + monthly 1st Monday schedule unchanged.

 55 - **Scout bot research task can occasionally hang silently for 1 request then r cover.** Session 55 second smoke test hung for 20+ min with zero progress logs, then a third attempt completed in
 7 minutes. Possibly a 409 Co flict overlap during rolling deploy. If re e ch s ems stuck, wait 2-3 min and retry; don't assume a code b g first.
 55 - ### Session 54 lessons to carry forward — from Session 53

 Session 55 summary

 fix-research-pipeline-contamination

 Session 55 summary

 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── fix-research-pipeline-contamination ──

 1. BUG 3 end-to-end close-out. Discovered F2 is NOT in the scheduled daily scan (Session 54 assumption was wrong). Built Telethon bridge, fired /prospect_add sentinel through live Railway bot, captured
 [BUG3_DIAG] log proving canonical 20-col write ( A1960:T1960 pos_of_strategy=[8] ). Reverted diagnostic commit 68622aa via 9b51a67 . Deleted 5 ZZZ sentinel rows. Queue stable at 1954/1954 canonical.

 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── fix-research-pipeline-contamination ──

 2. BUG 5 two-stage cross-district filter (6 commits + 1 fix commit + 1 audit commit). Plan went through v1 → v2 self-pressure-test → v3 Steven's ruthless 7-step prompt. Biggest architectural shift: filter at
 raw_pages boundary BEFORE Claude extraction, not after. Stage 1 page filter + Stage 2 contact filter + strengthened L10 + schema migration fix + L15 bypass fix. Kill switch ENABLE_RESEARCH_CONTAM_FILTER . Live
 smoke test on Lackland ISD: 9/234 pages dropped, 25/25 new rows fingerprint clean. Historical audit on 483 rows: 95% clean.

 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── fix-research-pipeline-contamination ──

 3. Telethon bridge + screenshot capability for future end-to-end automation. Claude Code can now drive Scout Telegram without Steven manually relaying messages.
